programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Naval intelligence ensign is killed in an unusual car accident. Gibbs and his team are reunited with old friend Ned Dorneget, who is now working with NCIS cyber operations, and NSA attorney Jake Malloy to investigate the case, and discover a conspiracy involving terrorist recruitment on the internet

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Special Agent Ned Dorneget of the NCIS cyber division joins the team to further their investigation into The Calling, the insidious terror network radicalising youngsters via the internet. When it emerges young suicide bomber Bradley was not, in fact, affiliated with The Calling, Gibbs and his colleagues realise they might be back to square-one. Determined to bring the organisation down, the agents pursue two new leads, an arms dealer who may be supplying The Calling with their signature German S-mines, and another boy given a deadly mission by the terrorists. Guest starring Matt Jones

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The death of Special Agent Ned Dorneget hits the team hard, and when Gibbs receives a visit from his late colleague's mother - CIA Officer Joanna Teague - he must decide whether to let her help with the investigation into the Calling. Meanwhile, Gibbs hopes his young charge Luke, formerly one of the Calling's radicalised footsoldiers, might be key to bringing the organisation down. However, when the terrorists discover where their defecting comrade has taken refuge, the abduct him and flee to Iraq. The team follows hot on their heels, but it seems Luke's ties to the Calling might be stronger than anyone at NCIS ever anticipated. Naval crime drama, starring Mark Harmon, with guest Mimi Rogers

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony and the team try to prove Ziva had nothing to do with a political assassination carried out by her former Mossad partner - but have to tackle the case without Gibbs, who has retired to Mexico and now spends his time building boats. Naval crime drama, starring Cote de Pablo and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Against his better judgement, Gibbs comes out of retirement to help Fornell and his daughter, whose lives are being threatened by an escaped convict. The team reopens the criminal's case and questions a former sailor, the only person to have contacted him while he was in jail. Starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette, with Hal Holbrook

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Part one of two. A virus is uploaded to Walter's computer, turning Team Scorpion's new 'smart' building project into a burning death trap - with people locked inside

programme.title: Scorpion

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Calvin Lee, an executive naval officer, appears to commit suicide by hanging himself from a public road bridge. When examining CCTV footage of the incident, Sam and Callen discover the dead man tossed away a small object before jumping to his death, and when they learn the late lieutenant commander had been working on a sensitive project involving new cloaking technology for submarines, they immediately take an interest in the item. The investigation draws the team into the furtive world of LA's Chinese community, where they discover Lee might have been harbouring some personal as well as professional secrets. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A naval officer involved in an illegal underground street race is killed when his vehicle explodes. The team's investigations reveal he was carrying classified equipment in relation to his work on a new form of battery that the military had hoped to adopt, and that the explosion - captured on CCTV - is not in accordance with that expected of normal fuel. Meanwhile, Callen latest driving misdemeanour means he must attend traffic school. Chris O'Donnell stars

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Marine gunnery sergeant Wendell Dobbs is gunned down in a drive-by shooting by assailants wielding AK-47s, and the team suspects the victim may have been involved in illegally importing automatic weapons into the country and supplying them to local gangs. Sam goes undercover to find the truth after learning Dobbs gave up his combat post to mentor his younger brother, who had been hanging out with a gang. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Marine Daniel Zuna is found dead in a pool of his own blood in an alleyway outside a night club, and the team's first stop is to investigate the mixed martial arts gym where the victim spent most of his off-duty time. Kensi poses as Zuna's new girlfriend and suspects the murder could be related to something that happened in Iraq, prompting Sam to go undercover as an MMA fighter in order to see if he can find out the truth. Crime drama, starring LL Cool J and Daniela Ruah

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A couple are taking in the view from a cliff top when a car driven by an already bleeding man crashes into theirs and teeters on the brink before plunging over the precipice. Callen and the team are brought in to investigate when the driver is identified as Lieutenant Brian Roth, and they discover that the car is registered to a Hollywood socialite Aubrey Darva. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Terry poses as a hitman in a plan to entrap a gambling shack owner determined to dispose of his wife's lover. The operation takes on extra urgency when it emerges the runaway couple have booked one-way tickets to Australia for the next day. Roger is furious with Lewis when the pair manage to lose a prisoner after the ambulance they are travelling in is involved in an accident. Emma's complicated love-life reaches crisis point when her ex finds her in the pub

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Will pays dearly for the satisfaction of punching Matt's lights out when Gina gives him a ferocious dressing down, the injured party files a charge and Emma starts to feel sorry for her ex anyway. To make matters worse, he's ruled himself out of the boxing tournament, leaving Dan to fly the flag for Sun Hill. Sam is shocked at Stuart's ruthless treatment of a suspect - a development not lost on Phil Hunter

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Feelings run high in the community when a 12-year-old suffers gunshot wounds but refuses to say who is responsible, leaving Adam to face the wrath of the estate residents. Lewis's rash decision to enter a squat puts Roger in a difficult situation, as he struggles to overcome the effects of post-traumatic stress. Will gets further encouragement from Emma, despite her ex's refusal to let her go

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Will gives up on Emma after being stood up and witnessing a smug Matt whisk her off for a drink. New trainee DC Kezia Walker finds a kindred spirit in mentor Suzie Sim, while Tony Stamp is embarrassed to be cornered by a ferocious dog. Adam finds his reaction to the furore over the shooting incident could jeopardise his application for promotion to Borough Commander. Ken Campbell guest stars

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: The elder Dr Bramwell plans to open a practice on Harley Street and proposes marriage to Mrs Costigan. Jemma Redgrave stars

programme.title: The Evacuees
programme.desc: Jack Rosenthal's 1975 drama, directed by Alan Parker, chronicling the experiences of two Jewish boys evacuated from London to avoid the horrors of the Blitz. Maureen Lipman, Margery Mason and Ray Mort star

programme.title: We're Doomed! The Dad's Army Story
programme.desc: Comedy drama based on the story of how Jimmy Perry and David Croft brought the sitcom Dad's Army to life. Running from Perry's initial idea in 1967 until the transmission of the first episode in 1968, the film shows the beginnings of their writing partnership and the casting woes, personal clashes and production difficulties that put the show's existence in jeopardy. Starring Paul Ritter and Richard Dormer

programme.title: Dad's Army: The Lost Episodes
programme.desc: Remake of the classic sitcom, featuring scripts associated with missing episodes performed by a new cast. Private Walker, source of the platoon's supplies of whisky, cigarettes and other essential black-market goods, is called up to serve in the regular Army, so Mainwaring decides to use all his standing as an officer and a bank manager to have him excused from military service. Meanwhile, Jones and the others try to ensure that Walker fails his medical. Kevin McNally stars as Captain Mainwaring, with Robert Bathurst as Sergeant Wilson, Kevin Eldon as Corporal Jones and Mathew Horne as Private Walker

programme.title: Dad's Army: The Lost Episodes
programme.desc: When he's told he can make a member of the platoon a corporal, Mainwaring instigates a competition between Frazer and Jones to see who deserves the promotion. Both do their best to impress him, particularly during a demonstration on how to face down a Nazi stormtrooper, but things go awry when a power-crazed Frazer charges the other men with court-martial offences. Remake of the classic sitcom, starring Kevin McNally, Robert Bathurst, Kevin Eldon and David Hayman

programme.title: Dad's Army: The Lost Episodes
programme.desc: Tipped off by Frazer and Godfrey, Mainwaring and Wilson investigate a house from where they believe someone may have been signalling to enemy aircraft. They take the suspect back to the church hall, but chaos ensues when an incendiary bomb comes through the roof and the platoon desperately battles the blaze. Remake of the classic sitcom, starring Kevin McNally, Robert Bathurst and Kevin Eldon

programme.title: dinnerladies
programme.desc: Norman the breadman fails to deliver, the stripper cannot play the accordion and Jean has bridesmaids' knickers on her mind. First episode of the canteen comedy, starring Victoria Wood, Julie Walters and Celia Imrie

programme.title: dinnerladies
programme.desc: The staff await an important royal visit, but first Stan has to pick just the right toupee, and Bren is determined to get the tables cleared away in time. Comedy, starring Victoria Wood and Thelma Barlow

programme.title: dinnerladies
programme.desc: Bren's mother wreaks havoc in the factory and incurs Stan's wrath by pursuing an ill-considered romance, while Anita organises a heated debate on the workers' current situation. Comedy, with Victoria Wood and Julie Walters

programme.title: The Two Ronnies
programme.desc: The two comedians host Christmas dinner, but someone steals the turkey and they must put their heads together to capture the thief. Comedy starring Ronnie Corbett and Ronnie Barker

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: A new midwife joins the team as the area is rocked by power cuts and blizzards. The new arrival is delayed by the weather and falls ill as soon as she arrives. However, she is quickly dragged from her sick bed to assist with a difficult birth. Nurse Crane and Dr Turner treat an elderly cancer patient who faces losing her house due to slum clearances, and try to convince Sergeant Woolf to allow her to die at home

programme.title: My Life Is Murder
programme.desc: After a near-death experience, Alexa is confronted by her own mortality as she tries to unravel the secrets of a murdered man whose body has been found in someone else's grave. Detective thriller, starring Lucy Lawless and Bernard Curry

programme.title: Rebus
programme.desc: An asylum seeker is murdered on a notorious Edinburgh housing estate in what appears to be a race-hate crime - but Rebus suspects there is something more complex behind it. A local MP also reports a missing Kosovan girl, who was last seen in a car with a known criminal - and it turns out he has vanished as well. Ken Stott stars as Ian Rankin's jaded detective, with Claire Price and Cora Bissett

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A university student who has committed suicide is revealed to be the victim of bullying, and concern grows when a gun is discovered in his locker and a memory card in his stomach is found to contain photographs of his tormentors. The team's fears become a reality when a building comes under attack by a rampaging shooter, leaving Harry and Nikki trapped inside while a helpless Leo is forced to watch on CCTV as the dramatic events unfold. Guest starring Phyllis Logan

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: Kevin rescues Sally from a blaze at a tower block, but receives a frosty reception, and the Watch is called to carry out a routine inspection of a strip club

programme.title: London's Burning

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine

Detective drama, guest starring David Bradley

Progress is slow, however, as the suspect's relatives prove reluctant to cooperate with the police. The rivalry between Phil and Stuart intensifies as they try to make an impression on Sam

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Faye opens up to Joseph about the death of her second husband, and Chrissie discovers Stuart is responsible for Sam's move out of cardiothoracics when she finds the lost patient notes in his locker. Donna is caught out over a deception designed to curtail the teasing about her love life

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Roxanne struggles to cope with the pressures of being a working single mother - with potentially grave consequences. Meanwhile, Duffy and Comfort are surprised to discover they both have an admirer, and Dillon competes with Simon for Heather's attention, clouding his professional judgement in the process. Another shift with the staff of Holby A&E, starring Cathy Shipton and Loo Brealey

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: The new man in Yvonne's life thinks she's a nurse until her work makes front page news in the local paper, and Mickey's relationship with Mia reaches a crisis as memories of the rape return to haunt him. Zain's underworld contacts lead him to an encounter with beguiling businesswoman Kristen Shaw (Christine Stephen-Daly), and investigations into the theft of rotten meat leave a bad smell as Heaton tries to implement a policy of proactive policing

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Pat is busy juggling work commitments and David lets her down. Della gets a shock when Steve loses his tether. Ruth gets good news leaving Michelle feeling inadequate

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: An unexpected invitation throws the Jacksons into confusion, while in the Vic Pat's courage is put to the test and nosy Nellie learns a few home truths

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: As Recall's wedding day approaches tragedy strikes Bluewatch when they attend a house fire. Starring Ben Onwukwe and Michael Garner

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Erica fears Andrew is getting too romantically involved with a doctor from a neighbouring surgery, and makes a surprise decision over his marriage proposal. However, while she's looking to the future, a dangerous legacy from the past is threatening public safety elsewhere in Cardale. Rural drama, starring Saskia Wickham and Gary Mavers

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: Paige's son turns up for an eventful weekend, which starts in the police station and ends in protest. As a result he has trouble finding the right moment to tell his father he wants to quit his medical studies. It isn't until they are drawn together in the fight to save a friend's livelihood that they can talk like father and son. Drama, starring Nigel Havers, Hugh Dancy and Jane Gurnett

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Chris comes up with a surprise, while Sharon's contentment is shattered by the medical advice all women dread - she has got to lose weight

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Tom sets out to prove himself by learning to drive and Harvey again applies for membership of the golf club, masquerading as a clergyman. Sitcom set in the Bayview Retirement Village, starring Stephanie Cole and Graham Crowden

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Alfonse is whisked to hospital with heart trouble after being interrogated by the Gestapo. Rene feigns a bout of food poisoning in a bid to rescue his friend before he spills the beans about the stolen money. With Gorden Kaye, Kenneth Connor and Carmen Silvera

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Howard secretly buys a van and asks Wesley to customise it for long journeys, but Compo insists on pushing the vehicle to its limits. Truly thinks Clegg is paying too high a tax, so he drags his friend to a council meeting. Auntie Wainwright has Smiler deliver a truck load of junk that the recipient did not order. Peter Sallis and Howard Fyfe star

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: The coffin of local poet Declan O'Grady falls from the hearse revealing that the corpse inside is not his but a young woman identifiable only by a notable scarlet tattoo. Funeral director Warren Bugle directs Mike, Kristin and Breen to Wadsworth Manor where a mysterious role-playing game was conducted the night before Declan's death.

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Brian finds the library card of cold case victim Dr Richard Symes, leading the team to reinvestigate the death of the professor, who perished after falling from the roof of his college three years' previously. Sandra and Gerry interview Symes' widow, who believes principal Jeremy Ventham drove her husband to suicide following a conflict over teaching methods. Crime drama, starring Alun Armstrong and Amanda Redman

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: A severed finger is brought to the Lyell Centre by DCI Rory Goodchild and immigration enforcement officer Pia Butler, who ask Nikki and Thomas to test for a DNA match with 17 year-old Syrian illegal immigrant Akka Khoury. The teenager was sent the digit by a people-smuggler who claims to have her mother and is demanding money for her safe return. Meanwhile, Akka's 12 year-old cousin Sosa is trying to enter the UK illegally hidden in a van on a cross-channel ferry. Emilia Fox stars

programme.desc: The Langridge family spend a trial week living in Perth, Australia. Abigail feels a new start would be great for the whole family, but her husband and son are not convinced

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: A mother and son take on a mother and daughter at the Royal Highland Centre in Edinburgh. Anita Manning leads the proceedings, with Paul Laidlaw and John Cameron helping the teams

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones visits Blenheim Palace to explore its private chapel and the grounds of this World Heritage site, while Laura Wright joins an inclusive choir for a hymn singalong

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore rescues two items from a recycling centre in Witley, Surrey, with creative duo Josh and Oli coming up with an idea that could make a fantastic profit

programme.title: Live International T20 Cricket
programme.desc: Isa Guha presents all the action from the second T20 in the three-match series, taking place at Emirates Old Trafford in Manchester. Prior to the opener, these sides had met in this format on 15 occasions, with England winning 10 of those contests, Pakistan claiming victory on four occasions and the other match ending in a tie. However, it was the visitors who prevailed by nine wickets the last time they played a T20 at this venue in September 2016

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit: A Grand Day Out
programme.desc: Nick Park's Bafta-winning film which introduced the animated inventor and his faithful canine sidekick to the world. Wallace is distraught when his cheese supplies run out, so he decides to take a trip to the moon. All he needs to do is build himself a rocket. With the voice of Peter Sallis

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: In a special edition, food writer Mary Berry takes over as guest editor to reveal how farming and the countryside have influenced her life and career - and still do. Matt Baker is with her to discover what has inspired her, from early childhood on her parents' smallholding to the rural issues she holds dear today. Adam Henson investigates the state of British pig farming, while Anita Rani visits a school with a field-to-fork ethos, which is something Mary would love to see more of

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Cromer pier in Norfolk, where items brought in to be valued include a strange machine labelled 'Certain Death', the Duke of Wellington's night cap, a note related to Oliver Cromwell, and letters from both Queen Victoria and John Lennon. Plus, Fiona is challenged to guess the values of three rare pieces of antique jewellery

programme.title: Strike
programme.desc: A frightening visit from a potential client puts a new case on the table and Cormoran Strike and Robin Ellacott set to work looking into reports of a strangled child. With their detective agency thriving, the duo are also recruited to investigate the blackmail of a government minister, with Robin going undercover in the House of Commons. The return of the crime thriller based on books by JK Rowling, starring Tom Burke and Holliday Grainger, with Robert Glenister and Robert Pugh

programme.title: The Accountant (2016)
programme.desc: High-functioning autistic number-cruncher Christian Wolff moonlights as a money launderer for the mob. However, the Treasury Department start closing in on him, so he decides to take on a legitimate client in the form of a robotics company. As he works through their books, he uncovers a discrepancy involving millions of dollars. Thriller, starring Ben Affleck, Anna Kendrick and JK Simmons

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Kate Humble meets a giraffe whose weight-loss could mean a crisis for the whole herd, while Ben Fogle finds out which eucalyptus leaves koalas like best and Jean Johansson serves up some refreshing fish lollies for the tigers

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits an former chip shop in Cumbria, Martin Roberts examines a one-bedroom flat in London, and Dion Dublin appraises a plot of land in Greater Manchester. This varied trio of auction lots are all ripe for reinvention, so the presenters pick the minds of their new owners to hear their plans

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Patients from Britain's most remote community - on Fair Isle, south of Shetland - wait for a weather window to make the trip to Lerwick for important operations

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and Raj Bisram help teams to scour an antiques fair at the Royal Welsh Showground in Builth Wells, with Eric Knowles overseeing proceedings

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Sabine's response dismays Mich when he asks for honesty, while ill-chosen words create a dilemma for Claudia

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts as children's TV presenters Hayley Hassall, Martin Dougan, Radzi Chinyanganya, Ayshah Tull and Katie Thistleton compete for charity

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps two brothers search for a house in Herefordshire with a budget of £800,000, with plenty of space for them to both live under the same roof

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The makeover show returns, with Charlie Dimmock and Dave and Harry Rich working for a Bristol couple want a garden that mixes Greek plants with English ones

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and James Braxton search the Home Counties for antiques, aiming to make the most profit when they sell the items at auction in Bourne End

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Paddington 2 (2017)
programme.desc: The lovable little bear has settled into his new life with the Brown family in London, becoming a much-loved member of the local community - until his search for a birthday present for his aunt leads to him being framed as a thief. While the Browns try to clear Paddington's name, he tries to make himself at home in prison, winning over the other convicts and getting involved in an escape attempt. Comedy adventure, starring Hugh Bonneville, Hugh Grant and Sally Hawkins, with Ben Whishaw providing the voice of Paddington

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a selection of eight of the most extraordinary sequences from Planet Earth II and Blue Planet II, with a new soundtrack by Hans Zimmer, Jacob Shea and the team at Bleeding Fingers - performed by the BBC Concert Orchestra and Mercury Award-winning rapper Dave on the grand piano. The film ranges from the East Cape of South Africa to the north of Norway and from Chile to the Indian Ocean to show how animals overcome adversity to survive and thrive in some of the world's most challenging environments

programme.title: Strike
programme.desc: Cormoran and Robin's startling discovery seems to confirm the story of the strangled child - but they're forced to leave the scene before they can properly investigate. Fearing their witness is in danger, they redouble their efforts to find him, leading them all over London in pursuit of the truth, from a derelict warehouse filled with protestors to a high-society charity fundraiser.

programme.title: Famalam
programme.desc: Prince Alyusi brings his brand of philanthropy to a wider audience, superhero Night Owl battles her most dangerous foe and the E19 Posse teach an important lesson. Plus, a golden ticket allows black people to be white for a day, and adventurer Wolf Chambers faces his toughest challenge yet

programme.title: Canada's Drag Race
programme.desc: RuPaul's Drag Race judge Michelle Visage is the guest host as the contestants attend a ball, with Brooke Lynn Hytes, Jeffrey Bowyer-Chapman and Stacey McKenzie deciding which three will make the final

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Comedian Katherine Ryan takes over the reins for extended edition of the satirical quiz from May 2019, with comedy writer Andy Hamilton and stand-up and actress Cariad Lloyd joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the stories making the headlines

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Cheetahs and endangered spiders both enter mating season. A Bactrian camel undergoes laser treatment, and a party is thrown to celebrate the ninth birthday of AJ the red panda

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits a property in Manchester and has some motorised fun with a toy car at a house in Liverpool. Plus, the team revisits a London project Lucy Alexander first saw three years previously. It didn't have a roof back then, but it's been worth waiting for the finish

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: The A&E staff have to juggle space when a patient arrives with mystery abdominal pains

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles starts this programme at the British Bird of Prey centre in the National Botanic Garden of Wales and asks the people of Carmarthenshire to help identify a mystery object. The red and blue teams then go shopping with experts Ochuko Ojiri and Jonathan Pratt, hoping to find items of value to take to auction in Cardiff

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Ryan has a problem with three little words, and Ash shares his fears with Sully

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: A team of names from the world of food and drink takes on the general knowledge champions, as Oz Clarke, Ed Baines, Paul Rankin, Jonathan Phang and Reza Mahammad hope to win a cash prize for their chosen charity. Hosted by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is on a property-searching mission in North Devon with a house buyer who has been tasked by her husband to find their perfect country home on a £600,000 budget

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Arit Anderson and the Rich brothers create a garden in Thatcham, Berkshire, for widower Lee, who wants a space where his children can have fun and play, but also remember their mum

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and James Braxton embark on the second leg of their five-part expedition, heading to Worcestershire, Warwickshire and the West Midlands

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which pairs of contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for the chance to compete for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat. Presented by Amol Rajan and Alex Jones

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Natalie Cassidy and Shona McGarty join Stacey Dooley to talk about Sonia Fowler and Whitney Dean, reliving classic scenes and revealing juicy behind-the-scenes gossip

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Part two of two. Michael Mosley and Mehreen Baig investigate the safety of cosmetic treatments for the body, including a procedure designed to get rid of cellulite for good, fat freezing and fat heating, and the rising use of anabolic steroids for body enhancement. The programme also examines the psychological impact of selfies on body image and puts the latest weight loss technology to the test against traditional exercise

programme.title: War on Plastic with Hugh and Anita
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and Anita Rani present an update on the campaign against single use plastic, challenging a British family from Berwick to go plastic free on a budget. Hugh discovers what becomes of the packaging for pre-packed sandwiches, and Anita conducts experiments on teabags to discover the secrets of their construction. The programme also examines how the Coronavirus pandemic has led to a rise in single use plastic for safety reasons

programme.title: Skin
programme.desc: Documentary following people with skin conditions undergoing treatment from leading experts. The patients in the first episode are Daniel, who has had a cyst in the middle of his forehead for the past four years, Chantelle, whose acne makes her uncomfortable approaching new people, and Amy, who has a port wine stain birthmark on her face, and is an ambassador for a charity helping people to accept similar conditions

programme.title: Skin
programme.desc: Patients in the second episode include Reanna, who has 25 warts on her hands and the back of her knees. Ivy is self-conscious about her acne and has been hurt by negative comments in the past, and Nick has alopecia, which he conceals behind an arsenal of hats, online avatars and self-deprecating humour

programme.title: Total Wipeout: Freddie & Paddy Takeover
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness provide the voiceover for classic clips from the adventure game show. Twenty brave men and women throw caution to the wind as they tackle a series of crazy obstacles - including the famous bouncy red balls - in a bid to make it through to the final three and the chance to win £10,000. In this edition, the contestants include a microbiologist, a PE teacher and a cheerleader

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: John Torode selects his favourite moments from the series as he reaches the end of his alphabetical search of the cookery archives for recipes from some of the nation's best TV chefs

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor are on the Somerleyton Estate in Suffolk to find out about an ambitious project aiming to turn East Anglia into a giant nature reserve. Adam Henson fears the worst for his winter barley as he gets harvesting

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: From giant gooseberries and growing the perfect sweet pea to flamboyant dahlias and plants pollinated by a single human hair, Monty Don celebrates the plant passions of some of the extraordinary people that the programme has met over the last few years. He also has plenty of hints and tips of his own to keep enthusiastic gardeners busy over the weekend ahead

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Brian Cunningham counts butterflies to better understand how to attract them to his garden, while George and Gill Anderson are in the allotment making the most of August's produce

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Rick Stein's German Bite
programme.desc: The chef embarks on a journey to unearth some hidden culinary gems, seeking out enticing fishing opportunities as he hunts for the best seafood from the north German coast. The trip also has a personal perspective for Rick as he travels to meet the German side of his family in the Rheingau

programme.title: Swallows and Amazons (1974)
programme.desc: Four youngsters go boating while on holiday in the Lake District where they encounter two more children with a boat of their own, and a rivalry develops over a series of escapades. Family adventure, based on Arthur Ransome's classic novel, starring Virginia McKenna, Ronald Fraser, Sophie Neville and Simon West

programme.title: The Lake District: A Wild Year
programme.desc: For the wildlife and people who live amid the stunning scenery of the Lake District, life is one of continuous change. This documentary features cutting-edge camera techniques offering a new and unique perspective on a turbulent year in the life of England's largest national park. Time-lapse photography shows months and weeks passing in seconds - snow and ice giving way to sunshine or the frequent rain showers - while the animals, plants and people find extraordinary ways to cope with the challenges of this ancient landscape. Narrated by Bernard Cribbins

programme.title: Rio Conchos (1964)
programme.desc: A shipment of rifles is stolen by a former Confederate general in the aftermath of the American Civil War, and he begins selling them to hostile Apaches. A cavalry major is assigned to bring back the weapons, aided by a mismatched band including one of his fellow officers, a duplicitous Mexican and a drunken former rebel. Western, starring Richard Boone and Stuart Whitman

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: An exploration of the history and culture of some of the most extreme locations in Britain. Nick Crane visits the Isles of Scilly, Ruth Goodman walks the dangerous path traversed daily by the Branscombe Cliff farmers in Devon, and Mark Horton investigates the Transatlantic Telegraph service. Meanwhile, Hermione Cockburn learns how the fossil of a large sea creature found at St David's Head, Pembrokeshire, shaped scientists' understanding of Earth's history

programme.title: The Millionaires' Holiday Club
programme.desc: The second of two programmes taking a glimpse into the world of high-end holidays. This edition follows Richard as he plans a big surprise for his long-time partner Martyn. Whether it's at their £10,000 a week private villa in Barbados or on the idyllic island of Mustique, the only challenge Richard now faces is picking the right moment to propose. Plus, Peter and wife Christine, who enjoy 'spending their childrens' inheritance' on exotic holidays, plan a trip to a top hotel in India, booking a suite once used by Prince Charles and the Duchess of Cornwall. Narrated by Eve Best

programme.title: Best of Top Gear
programme.desc: A final round-up of highlights from the last two series of the motoring show. Featuring the team embarking on a road trip across the highlands of Peru in classic American cars, and Chris Harris putting an all-electric race car through its paces

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: An eco-friendly gift-wrap business gets a helping hand from Santa, and a sock entrepreneur embodies the holiday spirit with his buy-one-give-one business model. A pair of candle entrepreneurs also try to persuade the Dragons - Sara Davies, Touker Suleyman, Deborah Meaden, Tej Lalvani and Peter Jones - that their products can spark some serious profit. But while all the hopefuls dream of Dragon cash for Christmas, these investors don't give the gift of investment unless a business has real sparkle. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer are joined by Feargal Sharkey for a trip to the River Lea in Hertfordshire, where Paul used to fish for perch with his friends when he was a teenager. Bob reads up on the wise words of 17th-century angler Izaak Walton and prepares a riverside picnic that brings back fond memories and leads to a reflection on the differences between teenage life in their 1970s youth and the present. Setting up home in a narrowbooat, he also prepares a dish Paul's mother used to make - corned beef and potato pie

programme.title: Harry Hill's World of TV
programme.desc: The comedian presents a guide to medical dramas, looking back at shows including Emergency Ward 10, Dr Finlay's Casebook, Casualty, Angels and Doctors. Harry assesses each show for the authenticity of sets and costumes, celebrates some of the most frequently recurring tropes of the genre, and reveals what it takes to make a successful hospital drama

programme.title: Liverpool FC: The 30 Year Wait
programme.desc: Documentary on Liverpool's Premier League title success, the first time they had tasted top-flight glory since the 1989/90 First Division campaign. The Reds were all set to clinch the title in record time, but the enforced break due to the coronavirus put their celebrations on hold, until they were eventually crowned champions in June thanks to Manchester City's defeat to Chelsea. Featuring interviews with key players and staff, this documentary focuses on what it means for the title to have finally returned to Merseyside after so many years

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Nathan is horrified by what Isaac's painting depicts and, as the artist's ominous premonitions begin to manifest themselves at Claire's homecoming, Peter races against time to save the cheerleader from Sylar's insidious evil. Drama, starring Hayden Panettiere and Masi Oka

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Niki's father pays her a visit which brings out her bad side, Hiro attempts to change the future of somebody close to him, and Claire joins the cheerleading squad in a six-month flashback to when the heroes discovered their powers. Fantasy drama, starring Ali Larter, Hayden Panettiere and Masi Oka

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: A further selection of highlights from the stand-up show focusing on a different topic each week. This time around, Rob Brydon, Sara Pascoe, Dara O Briain, Lou Sanders, Rich Hall, Dane Baptiste and others offer their thoughts on sex

programme.title: Spaceship (2016)
programme.desc: A teenager whose mother died in mysterious circumstances fakes her own abduction by aliens. Her father, still consumed with grief after seven years, tries to discover what has happened to her by seeking out her friends - who harbour strange obsessions of their own. Drama, starring Alexa Davies and Antti Reini

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: Police mount urgent searches for two people with dementia. Whilst out shopping with his wife, 83-year-old John turned his back for a moment, before realising that his 82-year-old wife Sheila has vanished on the busy streets of Gloucester town centre. After a frantic search around town, John calls Gloucestershire Police and a major search operation swings into action. Elsewhere in rural Gloucestershire, police scour lanes and wooded areas as they search for 79-year-old John, who disappeared from the home he shares with his wife, Marian when she was out visiting a family member for lunch

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Ben Fogle observes a Cuban crocodile death roll for the first time, while the keepers face a challenge from a pride of lairy lions and Jean Johansson meets some chatty parrots

programme.title: Call That Hard Work?
programme.desc: Poultry farmer Andrew, restaurant manager Angela and performer Jack go head to head in a bid to prove their job is the hardest. The trio get off to a cracking start sorting thousands of eggs on poultry farmer Andrew's smallholding in Berkshire. While performer Jack takes his new role a little too slowly, restaurant manager Angela finds herself in a total scramble. The next day, both Andrew and Jack struggle to memorise four menus as they prepare to serve 40 covers at the high-end Indian restaurant where Angela works. For their final day, both the poultry farmer and restaurant manager face their fears to perform in front of a 45-strong audience at top tourist attraction the London Dungeon. Narrated by Sunetra Sarker

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores the growth of Kuala Lumpur, from a swampland tin mine to a major British colony, before becoming a major economic powerhouse in its own right. He meets jewellery at the Royal Selangor pewter factory, samples Malaysia's national dish, and investigates the scandalous behaviour of an infamous British resident general at the turn of the 20th century. He then heads to Melaka, for centuries one of the greatest trading ports in the world, before finally arriving at the tip of the Malaysian peninsula in the historic city of Johor Bahru

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness, his wife Erin and their two children sample life in Melbourne for a week, hoping the move would improve their work-life balance and quality of family time

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: Curious Creatures
programme.desc: Kate Humble returns with the quiz that tests wildlife knowledge, with Countryfile presenter Anita Rani and vet Steve Leonard joining Chris Packham and Lucy Cooke

programme.title: It's a Mad, Mad, Mad, Mad World (1963)
programme.desc: A motley assortment of characters wreaks havoc across America in the search for loot from a botched bank robbery - but the only clue to the whereabouts of the fortune is the cryptic final phrase uttered by a car crash victim. Comedy, starring Spencer Tracy, Milton Berle, Dick Shawn, Jonathan Winters, Phil Silvers, Terry-Thomas, Peter Falk, Ethel Merman, Jimmy Durante. and Buster Keaton

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: The broadcaster examines the evolution of aggression in the animal kingdom, looking at the Siamese fighting fish, which is so aggressive it will fight its own reflection until it is exhausted, and the kangaroo, which has a kick so strong it can kill

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin and his team of experts invite members of the public to bring their antiques to be viewed and valued, with an option to sell at auction

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Andi Oliver, Ivo Graham, Phill Jupitus and Kate Humble test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week, with a winner declared each Friday

programme.title: Gardening Together with Diarmuid Gavin
programme.desc: Diarmuid helps Rachel Kelly in Drumbo, Co Down, who wants to get rid of an eyesore in her garden. He is joined by plant experts Paul Smyth and Colm O'Driscoll to offer advice, and reveals whether a huge greenhouse made of reclaimed materials has made the deadline in his own garden

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: TV presenters Anthea Turner and Andi Peters join experts Charles Hanson and Margie Cooper on a quest to find items to sell at auction. Their search takes them through Cheshire, Greater Manchester and Staffordshire, with Margie allowing Andi to indulge his passion for aviation when he swaps their classic car for a vintage Vulcan bomber, while Anthea learns about Staffordshire's rich heritage and why the county is synonymous with pottery

programme.title: Tom Kerridge: Lose Weight for Good
programme.desc: Tom turns the spotlight on the dieters in the group who say they are too busy, or too inexperienced to cook their own healthy meals. He shows the group quick and easy recipes they can make at home, including a 'cheat pizza', a pea and ham pasta dish and a low calorie French apple tart. Later, Tom reveals how much weight the dieters have lost in the first month of their weight loss journey

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The eighth first-round match sees four students from Darwin College, Cambridge pit their wits against a team from the University of St Andrews in a bid to reach the second stage. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: The Diagnosis Detectives
programme.desc: Michael Mosley challenges 12 of the UK's leading medical experts to make a diagnosis for patients whose debilitating symptoms have baffled other doctors. Paul's dreams of a happy retirement have been ruined by a mystery condition that has caused his face to swell up so dramatically that he sometimes struggles to see, while Judy struggles to eat solid food and is dramatically losing weight

programme.title: Molly's Game (2017)
programme.desc: The true story of Molly Bloom, an Olympic-class skier who ran the world's most exclusive high-stakes poker game for a decade before being arrested in the middle of the night by FBI agents wielding automatic weapons. Her players included Hollywood royalty, sports stars, business titans and finally - unbeknownst to her - the Russian Mob. Drama directed by The West Wing creator Aaron Sorkin, starring Jessica Chastain, Idris Elba and Kevin Costner

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor are on the Somerleyton Estate in Suffolk to find out about an ambitious project aiming to turn East Anglia into a giant nature reserve. Adam Henson fears the worst for his winter barley as he gets harvesting

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: A fibre artist, a ceramicist and an abstract painter create artworks for a Staffordshire who woman wants a piece to mark the birth of her first grandchild. Nick Knowles presents

programme.title: Mandy
programme.desc: It is dinner at Trent Manor, and everyone around the table has a motive for murdering Lord Trent. But who did? Mandy wasn't invited to the manor, so she is off doing something else. Comedy short, written, directed by and starring Diane Morgan, with guest star David Bradley

programme.title: Mandy
programme.desc: Mandy rings radio DJ Iain Lee and tells him how she became trapped in a coffin, having been forced into a robbery. Comedy short written, directed by and starring Diane Morgan, with Natalie Cassidy

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Kate Humble meets a giraffe whose weight-loss could mean a crisis for the whole herd, while Ben Fogle finds out which eucalyptus leaves koalas like best and Jean Johansson serves up some refreshing fish lolliies for the tigers

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The makeover show returns, with Charlie Dimmock and Dave and Harry Rich working for a Bristol couple want a garden that mixes Greek plants with English ones

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don shows how to prune spring-flowering roses and makes successional sowings of vegetables to ensure continuous crops. Adam Frost gives tips helping plants cope with drought conditions and shows how to save precious water. The programme meets two sisters from Dorset who have discovered a new love of gardening, supported in their efforts by many followers on social media, and in Bognor Regis visits a gardener who has used unusual materials and bargain plants to create her own dream garden. Plus, Frances Tophill heads to Hertfordshire to meet a couple of brothers whose gardens are designed with wildlife in mind and in

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Disillusioned by life in the NHS, nurse and mum Yvonne Samson wants to move from Glasgow to Australia's Sunshine Coast, but husband David is happy to stay in Scotland

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold introduces action from the latest fixtures, including Wigan Warriors v Catalans Dragons and St Helens v Hull Kingston Rovers

programme.title: Curious Creatures
programme.desc: Kate Humble hosts the panel show that tests the wildlife knowledge of team captains Chris Packham and Lucy Cooke and their guests Maya Plass and Lindsey Chapman

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Jack is found in his drunken state by Roy, who cleans him up before returning him to Ash Park. Henry and Sarah carry out the autopsy on Regina and make a startling discovery

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood's alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture continues with the letter M. Various BBC gardening presenters are mesmerised by magnolias, marvel at the monkey puzzle tree and meet a marigold enthusiast

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: A couple and their three children embark on a six-week experiment to discover how a revolution in food transformed the British way of life, guided by records of what people ate for breakfast, lunch and dinner in the decades after the Second World War. Under the guidance of journalist Giles Coren and food historian Polly Russell, they begin by sampling meals from the early 1950s, when rationing was still in effect and staples included dried eggs, national bread, dripping and liver. With a contribution by Mary Berry

programme.title: Animal Super Parents
programme.desc: Though it takes two to make a baby, it does not necessarily require two to bring it up. So what makes the parents of the animal kingdom decide to stay or go, and if they remain with what degree to involve themselves? Narrator Hugh Dennis observes creatures from a California mouse who has to kick-start her partner's paternal instincts to the Adelie penguin parents who cannot leave their eggs alone for five minutes, and from cheetah mums practising promiscuity to keep the dads on-side, to flamingo parents both producing milk for their young

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Experts James Lewis and Jonathan Pratt assess items brought to Dover Castle in Kent, while presenter Paul Martin looks at the life of James Bond creator Ian Fleming

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Andi Oliver, Ivo Graham, Phill Jupitus and Kate Humble test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week, with a winner declared each Friday

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Neil and Rhian from the Brecon Beacons and Crymych-based Mandy and Derek hope to beat the competition with their pedigree breed sheep at the Pembrokeshire County Show

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Call The Midwife co-stars Cliff Parisi and Judy Parfitt team up with regulars Catherine Southon and Phillip Serrell. Headed for an auction in Norfolk, they start their antiques hunt in Kent. Will Cliff's love for vintage toy cars secure the biggest profits or can Judy's tough bargaining skills see her triumph?

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: As autumn sets in, the farmers prepare for mating season. In Aberdeenshire, deer farmers Isla and George wrangle their herd of semi-wild deer closer to the farm to tackle the challenges of rutting season. In the Lake District, Matt and Murray invest in some new rams, while in Armadale, Joyce Campbell's 600 ewes get stuck in the mud while being herded to the hills. On the Stoer Peninsula, Helen and Graham take their small boat out during settled weather to collect their creel pots

programme.title: Manctopia: Billion Pound Property Boom
programme.desc: The West Tower in the city centre is the latest skyscraper to open to the booming rental market. General manager Matt has over 300 apartments to rent out for owners Legal & General, and starts off with free yoga classes in the penthouse to raise the tower's profile. But as towers rise on the skyline, Salford mayor Paul Dennett needs to find a way to build more affordable housing in the city. Homeless people and low-income earners are often forced into shelters or temporary accommodation as the city's affordable housing stock falls short

programme.title: They Came from Manchester: Five Decades of Mancunian Pop
programme.desc: Compilation of BBC studio performances by some of the city's most popular bands, ranging from the 1960s to the present day. Featuring footage of archive performances by Freddie & the Dreamers, the Hollies, 10cc, Buzzcocks, the Fall, James, Joy Division, M People and Oasis

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: Action from the third and final match in the series, which came from Emirates Old Trafford in Manchester. England had won their last five T20 series and were looking to end on a high today against a strong Pakistan side

programme.title: Best of Top Gear
programme.desc: The team looks back at highlights of the last two series of the motoring show, including Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness heading to the searing heat of Ethiopia in their first cars, and Chris racing the £2.5m McLaren Speedtail against the RAF's newest fighter jet

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace look back on further highlights from 15 years of the show, remembering some of the most entertaining team challenges - and how different dynamics can lead to either success or chaos in the kitchen. They also revisit a 2015 semi-final task which saw Sheree Murphy, Scott Maslen, Rylan Clark-Neal, Sam Nixon, Chesney Hawkes, Mica Paris, Kimberly Wyatt and Tom Parker thrown together in a Victorian-style village, using only ingredients widely available during that era

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein reinvestigate the 1884 murder of a policeman in Co Durham, a crime for which three men were tried but only one was hanged

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: The presenter examines how the car industry in Germany and Japan blossomed following the Second World War, becoming manufacturing giants that far outstripped the Allied nations that had defeated them. He test drives some of the vehicles Germany and Japan produced during the post-war period, as well as an assortment of other classic cars, including E-Types and Mustangs. He also reveals the sad tale of the Austin Allegro, a vehicle that had a devastating impact on the British car industry

programme.title: BBC Proms 2020
programme.desc: Sir Simon Rattle and the London Symphony Orchestra perform a packed programme, featuring star pianist Mitsuko Uchida playing Beethoven, works by much-loved British composers Elgar and Vaughan Williams, and a new composition by Thomas Adès. Suzy Klein presents from the Royal Albert Hall

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Nick Crane heads to his beloved Western Isles in Scotland to attempt a daunting, long-coveted mountaineering challenge on the Isle of Skye

programme.title: Van Morrison - Up on Cyprus Avenue
programme.desc: Highlights of Van Morrison's unique live performance recorded on his 70th birthday in August 2015, on the Belfast avenue he made famous through the iconic album Astral Weeks

programme.title: Van Morrison at the BBC
programme.desc: A vintage compilation of the multi-talented Northern Irish musician's most memorable studio performances

programme.title: When Bob Marley Came to Britain
Classic Albums
programme.desc: Concert footage, home movies and interviews in Jamaica give a background to the Wailers' LP Catch a Fire, which launched Bob Marley onto the global music scene. Record label boss Chris Blackwell reveals how he masterminded the overdubbing of the album for the rock market and Rabbit Brundrick (keyboards) and Wayne Perkins (electric guitar) tell how they were brought back in to add their parts to the songs

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Denise is shocked to discover the extent of Chelsea's troubles, and May looks for ways to get closer to baby Summer. Shirley is temporarily put in charge of the Vic

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: May gets things ready for Summer's visit, and Charlie and Brenda's date ends in disaster. Shirley is determined to be successful and there are some surprises at the football trials

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: May visits the Square to say goodbye to Summer, Brenda has a revelation for Charlie, and Stacey interferes with Jean's love life. Lucas decides to move to Walford

programme.title: Handmade in Africa
programme.desc: Following master beadmaker Jane Semanto in her Nairobi studio as she crafts a traditional Maasai wedding necklace, which is of great ceremonial significance

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene featuring the vibrant golden red glow of a campfire, complete with cowboy resting by the crackling flames

programme.title: Africa with Ade Adepitan
programme.desc: Ade explores Tanzania, Ethiopia, and Somalia, visiting a game reserve where efforts are being made to halt the decline of elephant numbers. He tours Addis Ababa with former Olympic champion Haile Gebrselassie to discover why Ethiopia's economy has gone from strength to strength in recent years, and discovers the impact of war on Somalia, meeting a former NHS doctor who has returned to the country to help rebuild

programme.title: African Renaissance: When Art Meets Power
programme.desc: Afua Hirsch explores the cultural history of Kenya, revealing how artists, musicians and writers have responded to its colonial past and asserted a new identity through art

programme.title: Africa's Great Civilisations
programme.desc: Examining the tremendous changes wrought in Africa between the 15th and 18th centuries, including the seismic transformation as West African kingdoms encounter European mariners travelling farther and farther south along Africa's Atlantic coast

programme.title: Africa's Great Civilisations
programme.desc: Henry Louis Gates Jr brings the story into the 19th century, when a fierce competition for resources and trade stimulated ingenuity, while also enticing European powers and inciting conflicts that would threaten the stability and well-being of the continent

programme.title: Britain in Focus: A Photographic History
programme.desc: Eamonn McCabe explores how photographers responded to historic events they witnessed in the first half of the 20th century, including the two world wars and the Great Depression

programme.title: Jigs & Wigs
programme.desc: Big Fat Gypsy Wedding dressmaker Thelma Madine takes on the world of the feis with a unique range of outfits for dancers

programme.title: Jigs & Wigs
programme.desc: After stepping out in style on Britain's Got Talent in 2014, the Innova dance troupe are brought down to earth when they head back to the beaches of Portstewart

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene featuring the vibrant golden red glow of a campfire, complete with cowboy resting by the crackling flames

programme.title: African Renaissance: When Art Meets Power
programme.desc: Afua Hirsch explores the cultural history of Kenya, revealing how artists, musicians and writers have responded to its colonial past and asserted a new identity through art

programme.title: Johnny Kingdom - A Year on Exmoor
programme.desc: The amateur cameraman's search for red deer calves and fox cub leads to a dangerously close encounter with an animal that no one realised was living wild on Exmoor. In his home village, Bishop's Nympton, where Revel Week is in full swing, he and his friends also gather to welcome a new landlord at a local pub

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints an autumnal scene, using natural shades to depict a worn-down farm building surrounded by golden grassy bushes and trees

programme.title: Ray Mears and Ewan McGregor: Extreme Jungle
programme.desc: Actor Ewan McGregor picks up a few tips from survival expert Ray Mears before heading deep into the Honduran jungle on the trail of a lost civilisation, thought to inhabit the Mosquito Coast. Guided by real-life Indiana Jones Dr Chris Begley they brave freezing conditions and biting insects, and must endure physically demanding tasks

programme.title: The Yorkshire Ripper Files: A Very British Crime Story
programme.desc: The documentary charts the arrest, trial and conviction of Peter Sutcliffe, and the legacy for the relatives of his victims and the survivors of his attacks. The film explores how the trial was jeopardised when witnesses were offered money for exclusives and how the front seats in the court were reserved for VIPs. Going back to survivors of the attacks and relatives of Sutcliffe's victims, the film also explores the legacy left behind by his crimes and finds out what it has been like to live as the child of a Ripper victim

programme.title: Julius Caesar Revealed
programme.desc: Mary Beard sets out on a quest to uncover the real Julius Caesar, challenging the received popular view of the Roman politician and general. The classicist looks at how he become the autocratic ruler of Rome, the tactics of his military campaigns, his use of spin and PR on his way to the top, and the reasons behind his assassination on the Ides of March in 44 BC. The scholar also examines his surprising legacy right up to the present day, revealing how his influence is still shaping language, politics and people's everyday lives

programme.title: The Stolen Maharajah: Britain's Indian Royal
programme.desc: The story of Duleep Singh, the last Maharajah of the Punjab, who was captured by the British army as a boy in the 1840s and raised in England as an aristocrat. The young man rose through British society to become a friend of Queen Victoria - but when he realised what had been done to him, he sought to reclaim his stolen Sikh heritage and became a rebel against imperial rule in India

programme.title: Art on the BBC
programme.desc: Picasso is known as the godfather of modern art. Art historian David Dibosa explores six decades of BBC archives to discover how TV has influenced our understanding of him. Dibosa reveals how filmmakers have portrayed the child genius who drew like a master, and the adult who rejected his conventional talent in a quest to reflect the world in ways that shattered all the rules

programme.title: How to Get Ahead
programme.desc: Writer and broadcaster Stephen Smith explores the flamboyant court of French monarch Louis XIV at the Palace of Versailles, where he surrounded himself with aristocrats, artists, interior designers, gardeners, wig-makers, chefs and musicians. Ambitious courtiers scrambled to get closer to the king, but the bedchamber seemed to provide a more unseemly path to power

They take part in a belly button festival, a giant sandal race and wooden washtub challenge in Japan; ride in tuk-tuk taxis in Sri Lanka; give it pedalo power and take part in a game of Kabaddi in India; test their Kung Fu talents in China; and show off their blowpipe skills at a tribal village in Malaysia! programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: A guided tour by 16-year-old Syrian boy, Ahmed, through one of the biggest refugee camps in the world - Zaatari in Jordan. Ahmed is like any other boy his age, except he's had to leave everything behind, including his toys and technology, to move to the refugee camp. Life in Zaatari is nothing compared to home. When the Syrian civil war began in 2012 his family had to abandon their affluent lives and flee across the border to Zaatari camp with thousands of others for safety. Now living in a storage container for a home after his house was destroyed in the civil war, he has lived in Zaatari for three years, along with 80,000 other refugees, who can't leave the camp without permission. Despite their camp having everything they need, from shops to a hospital, school and football pitches, life is very different to how it was before. Ahmed doesn't let this affect his positivity though, and he finds new ways to have fun with his friends - even if it is in a desert sandstorm followed by flood
