programme.desc: The friends help a man whose car is stuck in mud. Later, the Box pays the Twirlywoos a visit. Narrated by Linda Bassett

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Educational programme for children aged between four and seven

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Po eats Tubby Custard and gets a messy bib

programme.title: Olobob Top
programme.desc: Bobble cannot reach his balloon

programme.title: Pablo
programme.desc: The little boy has two pinecones for tapping, which turn out to be explorers. They lead the Book Animals on a journey to discover sounds and textures and Llama makes an aural map

programme.title: What's the Big Idea?
programme.desc: Hugo sees some robots fighting and learns that some people have to fight to defend others from danger

programme.title: Time for School
programme.desc: Observational documentary on the lives of reception children at two contrasting primary schools - one rural, and one urban

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Gem and her teams of Swashbucklers try to win her jewels back from pirates Cook, Line and Sinker. Game show for children

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Tino and Eugene are having a disagreement about an amateur dramatic show they want to put on. One wants an all singing all dancing extravaganza while the other, a serious drama. Can Duggee and the squirrels help them get something ready to perform?

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: Peter and his friends try to find the perfect place to eat the radishes they took from Mr McGregor's garden, but fail to realise a thief is hankering after the vegetables

programme.title: Andy's Prehistoric Adventures
programme.desc: Andy heads back 160 million years in search of a prehistoric clamshell and plunges into the waters of the ancient ocean to meet some giant swimming reptiles

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie Moate explores different ways to travel. She uses an underwater camera to see a boat propeller and an animation reveals how the propeller pushes the boat through the water

programme.title: Bitz & Bob
programme.desc: While on a treasure hunt on Faraway Island, Bitz and Bob have to rescue Purl from the clutches of Pirate Bevelbeard and his ship mates

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Cartoon following four adventure-seeking superheroes as they explore places all around the world

programme.title: Topsy and Tim
programme.desc: Tim arrives at Tony Welch's house for a playdate, where he helps Mr Welch with a DIY job in the garden and bonds with Tony's best friend Sam

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Fun for younger viewers featuring tales of the world's worst puppy who woofs and wags his way into the lives of an unassuming family

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo and Gran Gran fix up Gran Gran's old bike and cycle through the park together.

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Sarah suggests visiting the spare sounds section of the Big Shop when Duck loses his quack

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: While all of the friends stay busy in the little Toy House, Colly Wobble decides it's time to play a silly song. Everyone loves the silly song and they all dance along

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Small gives Tiny a sparkly bag and it is perfect for her panpipes, but when she takes them out of the bag, she finds it speaks, in sounds and shapes

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Upsy Daisy sings with a megaphone in the garden, and wants to carry on instead of going to bed or playing with Igglepiggle. Narrated by Derek Jacobi

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Orlando Bloom reads a story about a bedtime adventure in your dreams. From pirates finding treasure to visiting aliens on Mars, what will you dream about tonigh

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Roger Johnson and Rachel Burden present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Liverpool v Arsenal. Highlights of the curtain-raiser to the top-flight campaign, held at Wembley Stadium. The Reds secured their place in this match by winning their first title of the Premier League-era, while the Gunners defeated Chelsea 2-1 in the FA Cup final and prevailed 2-1 at Emirates Stadium when the sides last met

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: The Langridge family spend a trial week living in Perth, Australia. Abigail feels a new start would be great for the whole family, but her husband and son are not convinced

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: A mother and son take on a mother and daughter at the Royal Highland Centre in Edinburgh. Anita Manning leads the proceedings, with Paul Laidlaw and John Cameron helping the teams

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones visits Blenheim Palace to explore its private chapel and the grounds of this World Heritage site, while Laura Wright joins an inclusive choir for a hymn singalong

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore rescues two items from a recycling centre in Witley, Surrey, with creative duo Josh and Oli coming up with an idea that could make a fantastic profit

programme.title: Live International T20 Cricket
programme.desc: Isa Guha presents all the action from the second T20 in the three-match series, taking place at Emirates Old Trafford in Manchester. Prior to the opener, these sides had met in this format on 15 occasions, with England winning 10 of those contests, Pakistan claiming victory on four occasions and the other match ending in a tie. However, it was the visitors who prevailed by nine wickets the last time they played a T20 at this venue in September 2016

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit: A Grand Day Out
programme.desc: Nick Park's Bafta-winning film which introduced the animated inventor and his faithful canine sidekick to the world. Wallace is distraught when his cheese supplies run out, so he decides to take a trip to the moon. All he needs to do is build himself a rocket. With the voice of Peter Sallis

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: In a special edition, food writer Mary Berry takes over as guest editor to reveal how farming and the countryside have influenced her life and career - and still do. Matt Baker is with her to discover what has inspired her, from early childhood on her parents' smallholding to the rural issues she holds dear today. Adam Henson investigates the state of British pig farming, while Anita Rani visits a school with a field-to-fork ethos, which is something Mary would love to see more of

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Cromer pier in Norfolk, where items brought in to be valued include a strange machine labelled 'Certain Death', the Duke of Wellington's night cap, a note related to Oliver Cromwell, and letters from both Queen Victoria and John Lennon. Plus, Fiona is challenged to guess the values of three rare pieces of antique jewellery

programme.title: Strike
programme.desc: A frightening visit from a potential client puts a new case on the table and Cormoran Strike and Robin Ellacott set to work looking into reports of a strangled child. With their detective agency thriving, the duo are also recruited to investigate the blackmail of a government minister, with Robin going undercover in the House of Commons. The return of the crime thriller based on books by JK Rowling, starring Tom Burke and Holliday Grainger, with Robert Glenister and Robert Pugh

programme.title: The Accountant (2016)
programme.desc: High-functioning autistic number-cruncher Christian Wolff moonlights as a money launderer for the mob. However, the Treasury Department start closing in on him, so he decides to take on a legitimate client in the form of a robotics company. As he works through their books, he uncovers a discrepancy involving millions of dollars. Thriller, starring Ben Affleck, Anna Kendrick and JK Simmons

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Kate Humble meets a giraffe whose weight-loss could mean a crisis for the whole herd, while Ben Fogle finds out which eucalyptus leaves koalas like best and Jean Johansson serves up some refreshing fish lollies for the tigers

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits an former chip shop in Cumbria, Martin Roberts examines a one-bedroom flat in London, and Dion Dublin appraises a plot of land in Greater Manchester. This varied trio of auction lots are all ripe for reinvention, so the presenters pick the minds of their new owners to hear their plans

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Patients from Britain's most remote community - on Fair Isle, south of Shetland - wait for a weather window to make the trip to Lerwick for important operations

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and Raj Bisram help teams to scour an antiques fair at the Royal Welsh Showground in Builth Wells, with Eric Knowles overseeing proceedings

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Sabine's response dismays Mich when he asks for honesty, while ill-chosen words create a dilemma for Claudia

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts as children's TV presenters Hayley Hassall, Martin Dougan, Radzi Chinyanganya, Ayshah Tull and Katie Thistleton compete for charity

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps two brothers search for a house in Herefordshire with a budget of £800,000, with plenty of space for them to both live under the same roof

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The makeover show returns, with Charlie Dimmock and Dave and Harry Rich working for a Bristol couple want a garden that mixes Greek plants with English ones

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and James Braxton search the Home Counties for antiques, aiming to make the most profit when they sell the items at auction in Bourne End

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Paddington 2 (2017)
programme.desc: The lovable little bear has settled into his new life with the Brown family in London, becoming a much-loved member of the local community - until his search for a birthday present for his aunt leads to him being framed as a thief. While the Browns try to clear Paddington's name, he tries to make himself at home in prison, winning over the other convicts and getting involved in an escape attempt. Comedy adventure, starring Hugh Bonneville, Hugh Grant and Sally Hawkins, with Ben Whishaw providing the voice of Paddington

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a selection of eight of the most extraordinary sequences from Planet Earth II and Blue Planet II, with a new soundtrack by Hans Zimmer, Jacob Shea and the team at Bleeding Fingers - performed by the BBC Concert Orchestra and Mercury Award-winning rapper Dave on the grand piano. The film ranges from the East Cape of South Africa to the north of Norway and from Chile to the Indian Ocean to show how animals overcome adversity to survive and thrive in some of the world's most challenging environments

programme.title: Strike
programme.desc: Cormoran and Robin's startling discovery seems to confirm the story of the strangled child - but they're forced to leave the scene before they can properly investigate. Fearing their witness is in danger, they redouble their efforts to find him, leading them all over London in pursuit of the truth, from a derelict warehouse filled with protestors to a high-society charity fundraiser.

programme.title: Famalam
programme.desc: Prince Alyusi brings his brand of philanthropy to a wider audience, superhero Night Owl battles her most dangerous foe and the E19 Posse teach an important lesson. Plus, a golden ticket allows black people to be white for a day, and adventurer Wolf Chambers faces his toughest challenge yet

programme.title: Canada's Drag Race
programme.desc: RuPaul's Drag Race judge Michelle Visage is the guest host as the contestants attend a ball, with Brooke Lynn Hytes, Jeffrey Bowyer-Chapman and Stacey McKenzie deciding which three will make the final

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Comedian Katherine Ryan takes over the reins for extended edition of the satirical quiz from May 2019, with comedy writer Andy Hamilton and stand-up and actress Cariad Lloyd joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the stories making the headlines

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Cheetahs and endangered spiders both enter mating season. A Bactrian camel undergoes laser treatment, and a party is thrown to celebrate the ninth birthday of AJ the red panda

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits a property in Manchester and has some motorised fun with a toy car at a house in Liverpool. Plus, the team revisits a London project Lucy Alexander first saw three years previously. It didn't have a roof back then, but it's been worth waiting for the finish

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles starts this programme at the British Bird of Prey centre in the National Botanic Garden of Wales and asks the people of Carmarthenshire to help identify a mystery object. The red and blue teams then go shopping with experts Ochuko Ojiri and Jonathan Pratt, hoping to find items of value to take to auction in Cardiff

programme.title: BBC Wales Today Coronavirus Daily Update
programme.desc: The latest developments, including the daily Welsh Government news conference

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Ryan has a problem with three little words, and Ash shares his fears with Sully

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: A team of names from the world of food and drink takes on the general knowledge champions, as Oz Clarke, Ed Baines, Paul Rankin, Jonathan Phang and Reza Mahammad hope to win a cash prize for their chosen charity. Hosted by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is on a property-searching mission in North Devon with a house buyer who has been tasked by her husband to find their perfect country home on a £600,000 budget

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Arit Anderson and the Rich brothers create a garden in Thatcham, Berkshire, for widower Lee, who wants a space where his children can have fun and play, but also remember their mum

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and James Braxton embark on the second leg of their five-part expedition, heading to Worcestershire, Warwickshire and the West Midlands

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which pairs of contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for the chance to compete for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat. Presented by Amol Rajan and Alex Jones

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Natalie Cassidy and Shona McGarty join Stacey Dooley to talk about Sonia Fowler and Whitney Dean, reliving classic scenes and revealing juicy behind-the-scenes gossip

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Part two of two. Michael Mosley and Mehreen Baig investigate the safety of cosmetic treatments for the body, including a procedure designed to get rid of cellulite for good, fat freezing and fat heating, and the rising use of anabolic steroids for body enhancement. The programme also examines the psychological impact of selfies on body image and puts the latest weight loss technology to the test against traditional exercise

programme.title: War on Plastic with Hugh and Anita
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and Anita Rani present an update on the campaign against single use plastic, challenging a British family from Berwick to go plastic free on a budget. Hugh discovers what becomes of the packaging for pre-packed sandwiches, and Anita conducts experiments on teabags to discover the secrets of their construction. The programme also examines how the Coronavirus pandemic has led to a rise in single use plastic for safety reasons

programme.title: Hell On Wheels
programme.desc: Roller derby is one of the fastest growing sports in Europe. This documentary reveals the lives and loves of Wales' first and best roller derby team Tiger Bay Brawlers, following them through the biggest moment of their sporting lives - the European Continental Championships. To the uninitiated, roller derby looks like hell on wheels - a violent spinning vortex of tattooed women on roller skates, crashing into each other at high speed. To its devotees, however, it is more than just a sport

programme.title: Skin
programme.desc: Documentary following people with skin conditions undergoing treatment from leading experts. The patients in the first episode are Daniel, who has had a cyst in the middle of his forehead for the past four years, Chantelle, whose acne makes her uncomfortable approaching new people, and Amy, who has a port wine stain birthmark on her face, and is an ambassador for a charity helping people to accept similar conditions

programme.title: Skin
programme.desc: Patients in the second episode include Reanna, who has 25 warts on her hands and the back of her knees. Ivy is self-conscious about her acne and has been hurt by negative comments in the past, and Nick has alopecia, which he conceals behind an arsenal of hats, online avatars and self-deprecating humour

programme.title: Total Wipeout: Freddie & Paddy Takeover
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness provide the voiceover for classic clips from the adventure game show. Twenty brave men and women throw caution to the wind as they tackle a series of crazy obstacles - including the famous bouncy red balls - in a bid to make it through to the final three and the chance to win £10,000. In this edition, the contestants include a microbiologist, a PE teacher and a cheerleader

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Roger Johnson and Rachel Burden present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Liverpool v Arsenal. Highlights of the curtain-raiser to the top-flight campaign, held at Wembley Stadium. The Reds secured their place in this match by winning their first title of the Premier League-era, while the Gunners defeated Chelsea 2-1 in the FA Cup final and prevailed 2-1 at Emirates Stadium when the sides last met

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: The Langridge family spend a trial week living in Perth, Australia. Abigail feels a new start would be great for the whole family, but her husband and son are not convinced

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: A mother and son take on a mother and daughter at the Royal Highland Centre in Edinburgh. Anita Manning leads the proceedings, with Paul Laidlaw and John Cameron helping the teams

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones visits Blenheim Palace to explore its private chapel and the grounds of this World Heritage site, while Laura Wright joins an inclusive choir for a hymn singalong

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore rescues two items from a recycling centre in Witley, Surrey, with creative duo Josh and Oli coming up with an idea that could make a fantastic profit

programme.title: Live International T20 Cricket
programme.desc: Isa Guha presents all the action from the second T20 in the three-match series, taking place at Emirates Old Trafford in Manchester. Prior to the opener, these sides had met in this format on 15 occasions, with England winning 10 of those contests, Pakistan claiming victory on four occasions and the other match ending in a tie. However, it was the visitors who prevailed by nine wickets the last time they played a T20 at this venue in September 2016

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit: A Grand Day Out
programme.desc: Nick Park's Bafta-winning film which introduced the animated inventor and his faithful canine sidekick to the world. Wallace is distraught when his cheese supplies run out, so he decides to take a trip to the moon. All he needs to do is build himself a rocket. With the voice of Peter Sallis

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: In a special edition, food writer Mary Berry takes over as guest editor to reveal how farming and the countryside have influenced her life and career - and still do. Matt Baker is with her to discover what has inspired her, from early childhood on her parents' smallholding to the rural issues she holds dear today. Adam Henson investigates the state of British pig farming, while Anita Rani visits a school with a field-to-fork ethos, which is something Mary would love to see more of

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Cromer pier in Norfolk, where items brought in to be valued include a strange machine labelled 'Certain Death', the Duke of Wellington's night cap, a note related to Oliver Cromwell, and letters from both Queen Victoria and John Lennon. Plus, Fiona is challenged to guess the values of three rare pieces of antique jewellery

programme.title: Strike
programme.desc: A frightening visit from a potential client puts a new case on the table and Cormoran Strike and Robin Ellacott set to work looking into reports of a strangled child. With their detective agency thriving, the duo are also recruited to investigate the blackmail of a government minister, with Robin going undercover in the House of Commons. The return of the crime thriller based on books by JK Rowling, starring Tom Burke and Holliday Grainger, with Robert Glenister and Robert Pugh

programme.title: Van Morrison - Up on Cyprus Avenue
programme.desc: Highlights of Van Morrison's unique live performance recorded on his 70th birthday in August 2015, on the Belfast avenue he made famous through the iconic album Astral Weeks

programme.title: The Accountant (2016)
programme.desc: High-functioning autistic number-cruncher Christian Wolff moonlights as a money launderer for the mob. However, the Treasury Department start closing in on him, so he decides to take on a legitimate client in the form of a robotics company. As he works through their books, he uncovers a discrepancy involving millions of dollars. Thriller, starring Ben Affleck, Anna Kendrick and JK Simmons

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Kate Humble meets a giraffe whose weight-loss could mean a crisis for the whole herd, while Ben Fogle finds out which eucalyptus leaves koalas like best and Jean Johansson serves up some refreshing fish lollies for the tigers

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits an former chip shop in Cumbria, Martin Roberts examines a one-bedroom flat in London, and Dion Dublin appraises a plot of land in Greater Manchester. This varied trio of auction lots are all ripe for reinvention, so the presenters pick the minds of their new owners to hear their plans

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Patients from Britain's most remote community - on Fair Isle, south of Shetland - wait for a weather window to make the trip to Lerwick for important operations

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and Raj Bisram help teams to scour an antiques fair at the Royal Welsh Showground in Builth Wells, with Eric Knowles overseeing proceedings

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Sabine's response dismays Mich when he asks for honesty, while ill-chosen words create a dilemma for Claudia

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts as children's TV presenters Hayley Hassall, Martin Dougan, Radzi Chinyanganya, Ayshah Tull and Katie Thistleton compete for charity

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps two brothers search for a house in Herefordshire with a budget of £800,000, with plenty of space for them to both live under the same roof

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The makeover show returns, with Charlie Dimmock and Dave and Harry Rich working for a Bristol couple want a garden that mixes Greek plants with English ones

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and James Braxton search the Home Counties for antiques, aiming to make the most profit when they sell the items at auction in Bourne End

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Paddington 2 (2017)
programme.desc: The lovable little bear has settled into his new life with the Brown family in London, becoming a much-loved member of the local community - until his search for a birthday present for his aunt leads to him being framed as a thief. While the Browns try to clear Paddington's name, he tries to make himself at home in prison, winning over the other convicts and getting involved in an escape attempt. Comedy adventure, starring Hugh Bonneville, Hugh Grant and Sally Hawkins, with Ben Whishaw providing the voice of Paddington

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a selection of eight of the most extraordinary sequences from Planet Earth II and Blue Planet II, with a new soundtrack by Hans Zimmer, Jacob Shea and the team at Bleeding Fingers - performed by the BBC Concert Orchestra and Mercury Award-winning rapper Dave on the grand piano. The film ranges from the East Cape of South Africa to the north of Norway and from Chile to the Indian Ocean to show how animals overcome adversity to survive and thrive in some of the world's most challenging environments

programme.title: Strike
programme.desc: Cormoran and Robin's startling discovery seems to confirm the story of the strangled child - but they're forced to leave the scene before they can properly investigate. Fearing their witness is in danger, they redouble their efforts to find him, leading them all over London in pursuit of the truth, from a derelict warehouse filled with protestors to a high-society charity fundraiser.

programme.title: The Blame Game
programme.desc: End-of-summer edition of the comedy panel show, with regulars Colin Murphy, Jake O'Kane, Neil Delamere and Tim McGarry giving their views on the 'new normal', as they poke fun at how society is coping with the pandemic. The show has a slightly different, with some lucky viewers joining in from the comfort of their own homes. From the White House to the big house on the hill - will anyone escape the panellists' caustic wit?

programme.title: Famalam
programme.desc: Prince Alyusi brings his brand of philanthropy to a wider audience, superhero Night Owl battles her most dangerous foe and the E19 Posse teach an important lesson. Plus, a golden ticket allows black people to be white for a day, and adventurer Wolf Chambers faces his toughest challenge yet

programme.title: Canada's Drag Race
programme.desc: RuPaul's Drag Race judge Michelle Visage is the guest host as the contestants attend a ball, with Brooke Lynn Hytes, Jeffrey Bowyer-Chapman and Stacey McKenzie deciding which three will make the final

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Comedian Katherine Ryan takes over the reins for extended edition of the satirical quiz from May 2019, with comedy writer Andy Hamilton and stand-up and actress Cariad Lloyd joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the stories making the headlines

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Cheetahs and endangered spiders both enter mating season. A Bactrian camel undergoes laser treatment, and a party is thrown to celebrate the ninth birthday of AJ the red panda

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits a property in Manchester and has some motorised fun with a toy car at a house in Liverpool. Plus, the team revisits a London project Lucy Alexander first saw three years previously. It didn't have a roof back then, but it's been worth waiting for the finish

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: The A&E staff have to juggle space when a patient arrives with mystery abdominal pains

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles starts this programme at the British Bird of Prey centre in the National Botanic Garden of Wales and asks the people of Carmarthenshire to help identify a mystery object. The red and blue teams then go shopping with experts Ochuko Ojiri and Jonathan Pratt, hoping to find items of value to take to auction in Cardiff

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Ryan has a problem with three little words, and Ash shares his fears with Sully

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: A team of names from the world of food and drink takes on the general knowledge champions, as Oz Clarke, Ed Baines, Paul Rankin, Jonathan Phang and Reza Mahammad hope to win a cash prize for their chosen charity. Hosted by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is on a property-searching mission in North Devon with a house buyer who has been tasked by her husband to find their perfect country home on a £600,000 budget

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Arit Anderson and the Rich brothers create a garden in Thatcham, Berkshire, for widower Lee, who wants a space where his children can have fun and play, but also remember their mum

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and James Braxton embark on the second leg of their five-part expedition, heading to Worcestershire, Warwickshire and the West Midlands

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which pairs of contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for the chance to compete for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat. Presented by Amol Rajan and Alex Jones

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Natalie Cassidy and Shona McGarty join Stacey Dooley to talk about Sonia Fowler and Whitney Dean, reliving classic scenes and revealing juicy behind-the-scenes gossip

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Part two of two. Michael Mosley and Mehreen Baig investigate the safety of cosmetic treatments for the body, including a procedure designed to get rid of cellulite for good, fat freezing and fat heating, and the rising use of anabolic steroids for body enhancement. The programme also examines the psychological impact of selfies on body image and puts the latest weight loss technology to the test against traditional exercise

programme.title: War on Plastic with Hugh and Anita
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and Anita Rani present an update on the campaign against single use plastic, challenging a British family from Berwick to go plastic free on a budget. Hugh discovers what becomes of the packaging for pre-packed sandwiches, and Anita conducts experiments on teabags to discover the secrets of their construction. The programme also examines how the Coronavirus pandemic has led to a rise in single use plastic for safety reasons

programme.title: Skin
programme.desc: Documentary following people with skin conditions undergoing treatment from leading experts. The patients in the first episode are Daniel, who has had a cyst in the middle of his forehead for the past four years, Chantelle, whose acne makes her uncomfortable approaching new people, and Amy, who has a port wine stain birthmark on her face, and is an ambassador for a charity helping people to accept similar conditions

programme.title: Skin
programme.desc: Patients in the second episode include Reanna, who has 25 warts on her hands and the back of her knees. Ivy is self-conscious about her acne and has been hurt by negative comments in the past, and Nick has alopecia, which he conceals behind an arsenal of hats, online avatars and self-deprecating humour

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz has dressed Weasel up as a supervillain in order to pretend to save the city. Magoo mistakes his disguised neighbour for an actress in danger.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Weasel accidentally launches Fizz next to Magoo. The latter, believing he is driving through a wildlife reserve, mistakes Fizz for a lost child.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks he's underwater scooting and has discovered Atlantis. In fact, he's in New York, riding Fizz's latest invention.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: A heavy metal extraction platform carrying toxic waste at the bottom of the ocean experiences a devastating fire. Gordon rushes in with Thunderbird 4 when the rolling factory damages its steering capabilities. With the help of Lady Penelope and Parker on the surface investigating the dealings of dubious executive Hector Ambrose, boss of the company Hydrexla, Gordon sets about rescuing platform operator Ned Tedford and his plant Gladys and prevent the rig from losing its toxic cargo

programme.title: Mission Employable
programme.desc: Kids' documentary series looking at what it takes to become a professional at all kinds of exciting jobs. Agent Danny becomes a news photographer and it's his mission to take the perfect photograph to appear in a newspaper.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo is handing out food to the neighbourhood animals when he stumbles on a gigantic hamster that Fizz has released from some parallel dimension.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo can't find the book that Linda lent him and he's ready to turn the house upside down to find it. The problem is that he's actually in Fizz's loft.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo, thinking he's at the laundromat, wanders into a candy factory, where Fizz and Weasel are creating a bubblegum that makes Fizz-shaped bubbles.

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Things take a very unusual turn after the turtles are struck by lightning during a storm.

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Mike gets an idea of how he could impress Cindy after playing with Louise and her doll - he's going to stage a rescue mission.

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: A hungry stork moves in with Bean and starts eating him out of house and home. His solution is to return it to the wild - but first he has to teach it to fly

programme.title: NinjaGo: Masters of Spinjitzu
programme.desc: Having grown soft in the wake of their victory, the ninja are ill-prepared when a dangerous Serpentine sorceress from Master Wu's past resurfaces.

programme.title: Ben 10
programme.desc: Team Tennyson visits 'Adrenaland Jr.'

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Sunday Best!
programme.desc: Melvin Odoom, Una Healy and Luke Goss join Martin and Roman Kemp for the entertainment show, featuring music, chat and a host of fun and games

programme.title: The Voice Kids
programme.desc: Emma Willis hosts the grand final of the junior version of the talent show. Over the past eight weeks, the acts have been whittled down to four finalists, one chosen by each of the four members of the judging panel. Tonight, these acts compete to impress both their mentors, Paloma Faith, Danny Jones, Pixie Lott and Will.i.am, and the viewing public for the chance to be named this year's Voice Kids champion. The winner will receive a £30,000 bursary towards their musical education and a VIP holiday to Universal Orlando Resort

programme.title: Rolling in It
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a game show featuring a giant arcade machine, which three members of the public have to play to win thousands of pounds. In the fourth episode, Stephen is joined by guests comedians Jimmy Carr and Crissy Rock, and Diversity star Jordan Banjo

programme.title: Best Walks with a View with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Julia chooses six of her favourite and most accessible family treks from across the UK, in search of the perfect scenic walk that anyone can manage. She begins by heading to one of the most famous landmarks on the Jurassic Coast - Old Harry Rocks in Dorset, which boasts spectacular views towards the Isle of Wight and of the offshore chalk stacks, good opportunities for bird and butterfly watching and a rich variety of wild flowers in spring and summer. There are also miles of golden sandy beaches and a dramatic vista of the bay

programme.title: The Living Daylights (1987)
programme.desc: James Bond crosses the continents to help a KGB agent to defect to the West. While protecting him from an unknown assassin, the super-spy is drawn into the world of arms dealing and a plot to trade millions of pounds' worth of diamonds for weapons to supply mercenaries around the world. Action adventure, with Timothy Dalton making his debut appearance as 007, alongside Maryam d'Abo, Joe Don Baker and Art Malik

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth helps an old friend find refuge at country house Greenshaw's Folly, but a sinister shadow falls over the strange building's various inhabitants and a murderer strikes. Miss Marple uncovers secrets from the past about Miss Greenshaw as a dangerous storm gathers and the bodies start to pile up. Julia McKenzie heads an all-star cast that includes Jim Moir (better known as Vic Reeves), Kimberley Nixon, Fiona Shaw, Robert Glenister, Julia Sawalha, Judy Parfitt and Matt Willis (formerly of pop group Busted)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh returns with the celebrity version of the team quiz show. As ever, four star contestants are challenged to pit their wits against one of the show's ruthless quiz experts, in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds for their charity of choice. This time, Absolute Radio's Christian O'Connell, singer, TV presenter and vocal coach Carrie Grant, Channel 5 children's presenter Naomi Wilkinson and I, Daniel Blake star Dave Johns each try to race down the game board without being caught, before joining forces for the final chase

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent
programme.desc: Back in April, as the country went into lockdown, Ant and Dec presented this year's Britain's Got Talent auditions, knowing that the finals would have to be postponed until later in the year. Now the series finally returns, as judges Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon and David Walliams decide which acts will make it through to the semi-finals

programme.title: Britain's Favourite Detective
programme.desc: Sheridan Smith presents a countdown of the top 25 TV detectives of all time, as voted for by the British viewing public. From Marple to Magnum PI, Luther to Line of Duty and Cracker to Columbo, Sheridan takes viewers on a whistle-stop trip through 70 years of the most famous sleuths on television. From the spires of Oxford with Morse to the skyscrapers of New York with Cagney & Lacey, and from the quintessentially British Midsomer Murders to the brooding Scandi Noir of The Bridge, the programme flips through the crime files of the greatest detectives that have ever graced TV screens

programme.title: Fast & Furious 7 (2015)
programme.desc: After defeating Owen Shaw and his crew and securing amnesty for their past crimes, Dom, Brian and the rest of the team return home to live normal lives again. However Shaw's older brother Deckard - a rogue special forces assassin vows revenge on Dom, who he holds responsible for leaving Owen in a coma. Action thriller sequel, starring Vin Diesel, Jason Statham and Paul Walker in his last film role

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Action from stage two of the most prestigious race on the calendar, featuring a mountainous 186km route from Nice Haut Pays to Nice. programme.title: Motorsport UK
programme.desc: Richard John Neil presents action from Donington Park, with racing from Round Two of the Ginetta GT4 Supercup and round two of the Porsche Carrera Cup GB

programme.title: British Superbike Championship Highlights
programme.desc: James Haydon presents action from the second round of the campaign, which came from Snetterton. The Englishman won 11 races overall last season, as he went on to win the riders' championship by just five points over Australia's Josh Brookes

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show from locations around the UK, setting members of the public unusual challenges for a chance to win anything from the price of their petrol, to £1000 in the big finale game. This week, Stephen is in Nottingham

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Mandy is still determined to show Vinny that Paul cannot be trusted, while Cain returns from his trip, but his mood darkens upon discovering that Belle is back with Jamie. Billy is released from police custody and interrogated by Will, and Tracy encourages Nate to organise a family meeting so they can give everyone the news about the baby

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Johnny's past returns to haunt him when Scott tells Jenny all about the crime they committed more than 40 years ago, while temperatures rise at Speed Daal when Geoff turns up for work. Having returned from their meeting, Abi thanks Peter for his support, but Debbie is watching from a distance, with her suspicions heightened

programme.title: Mamma Mia! Here We Go Again (2018)
programme.desc: It's five years since Sophie invited her three possible dads to her wedding, and now most of her family are reunited as she prepares for the grand reopening of the Hotel Bella Donna. However she is upset because two of her fathers are unable to make it to and she is having trouble in her relationship with Sky. Abba-themed musical sequel, starring Amanda Seyfried, Lily James, Pierce Brosnan, Cher and Andy Garcia

programme.title: Parveen's Indian Kitchen

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Zoe, Matt, Mike and Wendy pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: After reaching breaking point with being followed around by Buddy, Martin resolves to find a vet to have it put down, while Ruth finds out that Bert has been trying to make his own whisky. Clive is suspicious of Mrs Tishell and who she might be cooking meals for, Penhale makes a hash of asking Janice out, and Louisa and Martin's therapy homework results in a picnic on the beach, where they are interrupted by local vet Angela Sim - who appears to be hallucinating. Comedy drama, guest starring Caroline Quentin

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh presents the first in a series of special episodes in which he reveals the secrets behind some of his favourite garden designs, and looks back on some of the most memorable assignments featured on the show. The first episode focuses on country gardens, and revisits three past projects the team put inventive spins on - a classic country garden, a design with a cottage twist, and finally a project that set out to create an enchanted atmosphere inspired by classic children's book The Secret Garden

programme.title: Sheridan Smith: Becoming Mum
programme.desc: Documentary following the actress through the course of her pregnancy, as she reflects on her past mental health struggles and fears she may develop post-natal depression. The programme follows Sheridan and her partner as she attends her first scans and pre-natal classes, and meets a psychiatrist who specialises in mental health during and after pregnancy

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Prince Charles: Inside the Duchy of Cornwall
programme.desc: Part two of two. The documentary examines Charles's legacy for the Duchy, as senior staff prepare William for the day that he succeeds his father as the next Duke of Cornwall. The documentary also examines his well-known passion for trees and traditional rural crafts, and how these may shape the Duchy's future

programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts a revival of the supermarket-based game show in which three pairs of shoppers go wild in the aisles to win a £3,000 prize

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz has dressed Weasel up as a supervillain in order to pretend to save the city. Magoo mistakes his disguised neighbour for an actress in danger.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Weasel accidentally launches Fizz next to Magoo. The latter, believing he is driving through a wildlife reserve, mistakes Fizz for a lost child.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks he's underwater scooting and has discovered Atlantis. In fact, he's in New York, riding Fizz's latest invention.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: A heavy metal extraction platform carrying toxic waste at the bottom of the ocean experiences a devastating fire. Gordon rushes in with Thunderbird 4 when the rolling factory damages its steering capabilities. With the help of Lady Penelope and Parker on the surface investigating the dealings of dubious executive Hector Ambrose, boss of the company Hydrexla, Gordon sets about rescuing platform operator Ned Tedford and his plant Gladys and prevent the rig from losing its toxic cargo

programme.title: Mission Employable
programme.desc: Kids' documentary series looking at what it takes to become a professional at all kinds of exciting jobs. Agent Danny becomes a news photographer and it's his mission to take the perfect photograph to appear in a newspaper.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo is handing out food to the neighbourhood animals when he stumbles on a gigantic hamster that Fizz has released from some parallel dimension.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo can't find the book that Linda lent him and he's ready to turn the house upside down to find it. The problem is that he's actually in Fizz's loft.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo, thinking he's at the laundromat, wanders into a candy factory, where Fizz and Weasel are creating a bubblegum that makes Fizz-shaped bubbles.

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Things take a very unusual turn after the turtles are struck by lightning during a storm.

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Mike gets an idea of how he could impress Cindy after playing with Louise and her doll - he's going to stage a rescue mission.

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: A hungry stork moves in with Bean and starts eating him out of house and home. His solution is to return it to the wild - but first he has to teach it to fly

programme.title: NinjaGo: Masters of Spinjitzu
programme.desc: Having grown soft in the wake of their victory, the ninja are ill-prepared when a dangerous Serpentine sorceress from Master Wu's past resurfaces.

programme.title: Ben 10
programme.desc: Team Tennyson visits 'Adrenaland Jr.'

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Sunday Best!
programme.desc: Melvin Odoom, Una Healy and Luke Goss join Martin and Roman Kemp for the entertainment show, featuring music, chat and a host of fun and games

programme.title: The Voice Kids
programme.desc: Emma Willis hosts the grand final of the junior version of the talent show. Over the past eight weeks, the acts have been whittled down to four finalists, one chosen by each of the four members of the judging panel. Tonight, these acts compete to impress both their mentors, Paloma Faith, Danny Jones, Pixie Lott and Will.i.am, and the viewing public for the chance to be named this year's Voice Kids champion. The winner will receive a £30,000 bursary towards their musical education and a VIP holiday to Universal Orlando Resort

programme.title: Rolling in It
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a game show featuring a giant arcade machine, which three members of the public have to play to win thousands of pounds. In the fourth episode, Stephen is joined by guests comedians Jimmy Carr and Crissy Rock, and Diversity star Jordan Banjo

programme.title: Best Walks with a View with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Julia chooses six of her favourite and most accessible family treks from across the UK, in search of the perfect scenic walk that anyone can manage. She begins by heading to one of the most famous landmarks on the Jurassic Coast - Old Harry Rocks in Dorset, which boasts spectacular views towards the Isle of Wight and of the offshore chalk stacks, good opportunities for bird and butterfly watching and a rich variety of wild flowers in spring and summer. There are also miles of golden sandy beaches and a dramatic vista of the bay

programme.title: The Living Daylights (1987)
programme.desc: James Bond crosses the continents to help a KGB agent to defect to the West. While protecting him from an unknown assassin, the super-spy is drawn into the world of arms dealing and a plot to trade millions of pounds' worth of diamonds for weapons to supply mercenaries around the world. Action adventure, with Timothy Dalton making his debut appearance as 007, alongside Maryam d'Abo, Joe Don Baker and Art Malik

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth helps an old friend find refuge at country house Greenshaw's Folly, but a sinister shadow falls over the strange building's various inhabitants and a murderer strikes. Miss Marple uncovers secrets from the past about Miss Greenshaw as a dangerous storm gathers and the bodies start to pile up. Julia McKenzie heads an all-star cast that includes Jim Moir (better known as Vic Reeves), Kimberley Nixon, Fiona Shaw, Robert Glenister, Julia Sawalha, Judy Parfitt and Matt Willis (formerly of pop group Busted)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special

programme.title: The Masked Singer US
programme.desc: Nick Cannon hosts the final of the celebrity singing contest, with the three remaining mystery stars to be crowned this year's winner. programme.title: Live British Touring Car Championship
programme.desc: Steve Rider and Louise Goodman present coverage of the fourth round of the season, featuring the 10th, 11th and 12th races of the campaign. Last season Rory Butcher claimed pole and victory in race one on his home circuit and the local favourite, now with Motorbase, will be hoping for another strong display. programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Richard Abernethie is found dead shortly after disinheriting his sole heir and specifying that his legacy is to be divided equally among the remaining family members. Poirot is called in by his solicitor friend Gilbert Entwhistle, to ascertain whether the late Mr Abernethie met with foul play - and also finds time to investigate the murder of a beneficiary under the new terms of the will. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Robert Bathurst, Anna Calder-Marshall and Geraldine James

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) and four known killers are among those who meet Poirot at a bridge evening arranged by wealthy adventurer Shaitana, who sits out the first game - only to be found later stabbed through the heart. As nobody has left or entered the victim's apartment during the night, the guests combine their skills to discover which of the party is responsible. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Lesley Manville and Alex Jennings

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present coverage of four races from Goodwood, including the 3.35 Supreme Stakes. Plus, three races from Perth, including the bet365 Gold Cup. With analysis from Jason Weaver and Mick FitzGerald, reports by Rishi Persad and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: As the Ropers settle into their new home, George realises he is going to have to look for a job. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: The Great St Trinian's Train Robbery (1966)
programme.desc: A crook and his cronies stash the money from a train heist in a deserted old mansion, planning to recover it in good time. However, several years later, when the thieves arrive to collect their loot, they discover the building has been turned into a school for girls where the pupils have become infamous for their escapades. Comedy, starring Frankie Howerd, Dora Bryan, Reg Varney and George Cole

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: After enjoying a trip to the theatre with Captain Hastings, the detective is disturbed by the sound of gunshots in an apartment at Whitehaven Mansions, and soon finds himself investigating a murder. Drama, starring David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson

programme.title: Princess Diana: A Life After Death
programme.desc: Diana, Princess of Wales' death set the British nation thinking about the monarchy, and a strange atmosphere existed throughout the nation in the unstable summer of 1997. Prime minister Tony Blair had just won an election with a landslide majority and the tabloid press were pursuing Diana everywhere. Events were already leading to a full-scale crisis and the sudden and unexpected death of Diana changed the country

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Foundlings are people who were abandoned as babies, often in the first hours and days of their lives and have had no way to unlock the secrets of their past. In this 90-minute special presented by Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell, new DNA technology and painstaking detective work is combined to help solve the mysteries of people with unknown beginning. The team uses an extraordinary new technique to try to identify birth families and find answers for people who have spent decades striving to find out where they came from

programme.title: Sanditon
programme.desc: Andrew Davies's adaptation of Jane Austen's unfinished final novel about a spirited young woman who finds herself in a seaside resort on the cusp of dramatic change - and becomes involved in the scheming of its ambitious inhabitants. Charlotte and Sidney find themselves at loggerheads but must work together in an attempt to rescue Miss Lambe. Tom tries to save Sanditon and his marriage, while Edward and Clara enter into a scandalous pact to finally get their hands on Lady Denham's inheritance

programme.title: Vanity Fair
programme.desc: Becky masters the fine art of living well on nothing a year, proving that a respectable public appearance is all you need to get credit. Her flirtation with Lord Steyne at least provides a reliable source of income, but the Sedley family are less fortunate. With her finances in tatters, a devastated Amelia is forced to send her son to live with the Osbournes. Becky rises to the very top of high society, even securing a royal audience - but Steyne expects something in return for his patronage, and when Rawdon is accosted by bailiffs, he seizes his chance

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Ronnie and Sam are given 10 hours to find a hostage who was taken by two armed men, following a bungled robbery in which an innocent 'have-a-go-hero' was shot dead. When one of the accused is seized, the team is offered a deal - he will tell them the location of the captive in return for immunity from prosecution. Tamzin Outhwaite guest stars as uncompromising lawyer Miriam Pescatore

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks is forced to reopen a 14-year-old case when a dying crack addict confesses to the murder of teenager Amanda Bennet, a crime for which her father Simon (guest star Tim McInnerny) was originally convicted. Brooks becomes torn between the belief of his former partner Bernie Rawlins and mounting doubts over the ability of current colleague Casey, and the case unearths the possibility that more ghosts from his drinking days will come back to haunt him. Starring Bradley Walsh and Paul Nicholls

programme.title: Davina McCall: Life at the Extreme
programme.desc: Davina travels to the Azores in the mid-Atlantic to investigate how animals cope with atmospheric pressure changes underwater, particularly the ones that have to take in air before they submerge. She swims with a school of dolphins and finds out about the inbuilt response that both animals and humans have when they go underwater, and meets a world champion freediver who teaches her the techniques needed to swim alongside sperm whales, before trying to overcome her nerves and dive down to the ocean floor on a single breath

programme.desc: A cleaner is found dead in her home and suspicion immediately falls upon her lodger, who is arrested and convicted of her murder. It appears to be an open-and-shut case, but Superintendent Spence begins to question the man's guilt and invites Poirot to help uncover the identity of the real killer. The Belgian sleuth teams up with crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) as he tries to crack the case. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Richard Hope, Joe Absolom, Amanda Root, Sian Phillips and Raquel Cassidy

programme.title: Midsomer Murders

The victim is discovered to be an undercover journalist, who had some unusual questions to ask about the village and the country house where the historical event took place. programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: The murder of a caretaker has been so carefully planned and executed that the police are left without a single piece of evidence. Adding to the mystery is the fact that the crime is laid out in detail in a manual purchased three weeks earlier in the victim's name. Brooks and Casey eventually track down the killer, who confesses, but the trial is put in jeopardy by a canny defence lawyer. Guest starring Toby Stephens

Brian Cox guest stars, with John Thaw and Kevin Whately

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Betty is appalled by Zoe's treatment of Paddy. Vic tells Viv how hard he has been trying to find Kelly. Dee asks Rachel to be a witness at her wedding

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Vic fawns over Viv after they spend the night together. Zoe is upset at the thought of Chris becoming bankrupt. Jack shocks Sarah by selling Woodside Farm for a profit

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Merton faces a race against time to stop a saboteur when Aidensfield hosts a prestigious car hill-climb. Meanwhile, Bernie finds an old car in a cowshed and decides to renovate it, giving Vernon his chance to scoop the winnings in the event. Rural police drama, starring Duncan Bell, Peter Benson and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: The Abbotts catch flu and Sid is forced to act as nursemaid. Sitcom, starring Sid James

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: A wartime memento prompts Sid and Jean to visit Paris. Comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: The Ropers' television breaks down, and fixing it is a question of whether the British Museum has the parts in stock. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A cleaner is found dead in her home and suspicion immediately falls upon her lodger, who is arrested and convicted of her murder. It appears to be an open-and-shut case, but Superintendent Spence begins to question the man's guilt and invites Poirot to help uncover the identity of the real killer. The Belgian sleuth teams up with crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) as he tries to crack the case. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Richard Hope, Joe Absolom, Amanda Root, Sian Phillips and Raquel Cassidy

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A seemingly straightforward burglary investigation takes an unexpected turn when the victim is found to be leading a double life and suspicions are raised that the break-in could have been part of an insurance fraud. Blaketon falls for the charms of a solicitor and teams up with Ventress to help her track down the beneficiaries of an unusual bequest. Rural police drama, starring Derek Fowlds

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The Sugdens look forward to moving into their new farm. Eric asks Zak to be his best man. Dee's hen night ends in tears after the other ladies share secrets about Pollard

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The stag night prank played on Eric makes him late for his wedding, and then his honeymoon has an unexpected hitch. The Sugdens have a meeting with Andy's teacher

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Mavis tries to make amends with Derek. The Platts discover Nick is in Leeds. Jack gives Fred free drinks in the hope of being made a Square Dealer

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kevin finds his new job boring while Sally enjoys running the factory. Des and Claire start their boating holiday and Becky changes her mind about not coming with them

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Schoolgirl Jennifer Sutcliffe becomes embroiled in a mystery when priceless jewels are hidden among her belongings. The precious stones once belonged to a prince who fell foul of a revolution, and now his cousin is a pupil at the same school as Jennifer, where Poirot finds himself drawn into the unlikely situation and realises nothing is quite what it seems. David Suchet stars as the Belgian sleuth, with Harriet Walter and Claire Skinner

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A seemingly straightforward burglary investigation takes an unexpected turn when the victim is found to be leading a double life and suspicions are raised that the break-in could have been part of an insurance fraud. Blaketon falls for the charms of a solicitor and teams up with Ventress to help her track down the beneficiaries of an unusual bequest. Rural police drama, starring Derek Fowlds

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: English country squire Marcus Waverly receives a series of anonymous letters threatening to kidnap his son Johnnie, but the case is dismissed by Scotland Yard - prompting Poirot to open his own investigation. Drama, starring David Suchet and Geoffrey Bateman

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Musicians compete for a prestigious award at the annual Thassingham Classical Music Festival - and Barnaby and Winter are called in when the winner is brutally murdered and his priceless violin is stolen. Suspicion rapidly falls on the maestro organising the festival (James Fleet), who had planned to debut his new composition at the event and has stirred up resentment by turning a community festival into a corporate one - while many suspect that the murder is the result of a curse. However, a drunken viola player (Simon Callow) turns out to have some dark secrets that may implicate him in the crime

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A murder inquiry is launched when the police are alerted to shocking internet footage showing a teenage girl being attacked and shot. The investigation is hindered by the lack of a body, but the video provides enough clues to eventually put a name to the victim and track down her killers. However, a vow of silence between the defendants means Jake and Alesha face one of their toughest court battles yet

programme.title: Cleaning Up
programme.desc: Sam's money-making plan starts to bear fruit, but it comes at a great cost. Frustrated by her mum's constant absence and the new lodger living in her bedroom, Alice seeks refuge at her dad's house. Later, Sam's listening device is found in the office, and an investigation is launched which cuts off their source of information. Desperate to make amends with Jess and bring in some money, Sam follows an inside lead and enlists the help of a fellow cleaner, Mina, to execute a risky scam in another office

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A homicidal rapist escapes from prison and embarks on a deadly game of cat and mouse with Morse, relying on his mastery of disguise and bizarre occult connections to stay one step ahead of the law. Only the madman's psychiatrist seems able to provide a lead as to his whereabouts - and her life is in mortal danger as a result. John Thaw stars, with Kevin Whately, Keith Allen, Richard Griffiths and Harriet Walter

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective begins to suspect something is amiss when he visits one of his favoured haunts in London for dinner and discovers a fellow regular has dramatically changed his diet. Down in Brighton, elderly Anthony Gascoigne lies dying while his estranged twin brother Henry falls to his death - but nobody is sure whether the man was deliberately pushed. Bizarrely, the key to the riddle seems to lie in a blackberry pie. David Suchet stars in the title role, with Philip Jackson, Hugh Fraser and Pauline Moran

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid agrees to buy Mike whatever he wants for his birthday - but is shocked by his request. Vintage comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Peter gets in the way of Abi's morphine fix, and, desperately worried about Todd, Sean searches various social media sites hoping for clues - but is left frustrated. Nicky puts Daniel in touch with another escort

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Carla is suspicious when Peter lies to her about going to meet Abi, and when she finds out he is helping her with her addiction, she warns him to be careful. When Gemma presents Paul with a signed photo of PJ and Duncan, Paul can hardly believe his eyes, while Jenny greets Johnny's return with hostility

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Johnny tries to win Jenny round assuring her that he needed time alone and it won't happen again, but Scott tells Johnny it is about time they had a proper chat. Debbie senses Abi's reluctance to spend time with her, and Daniel encounters Nicky while she is out with a client

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Belle shocks the Dingles, Vinny hits a nerve and Tracy puts pressure on Nate

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Marlon and Bob interfere, Nate reassures Tracy, while Paul cooks things up - but will it work?

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Victoria gets sad news, Belle grapples with her guilt when she sees Moira struggling and Tracy asks more questions

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Richard Abernethie is found dead shortly after disinheriting his sole heir and specifying that his legacy is to be divided equally among the remaining family members. Poirot is called in by his solicitor friend Gilbert Entwhistle, to ascertain whether the late Mr Abernethie met with foul play - and also finds time to investigate the murder of a beneficiary under the new terms of the will. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Robert Bathurst, Anna Calder-Marshall and Geraldine James

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) and four known killers are among those who meet Poirot at a bridge evening arranged by wealthy adventurer Shaitana, who sits out the first game - only to be found later stabbed through the heart. As nobody has left or entered the victim's apartment during the night, the guests combine their skills to discover which of the party is responsible. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Lesley Manville and Alex Jennings

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present coverage of four races from Goodwood, including the 3.35 Supreme Stakes. Plus, three races from Perth, including the bet365 Gold Cup. With analysis from Jason Weaver and Mick FitzGerald, reports by Rishi Persad and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: As the Ropers settle into their new home, George realises he is going to have to look for a job. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: The Great St Trinian's Train Robbery (1966)
programme.desc: A crook and his cronies stash the money from a train heist in a deserted old mansion, planning to recover it in good time. However, several years later, when the thieves arrive to collect their loot, they discover the building has been turned into a school for girls where the pupils have become infamous for their escapades. Comedy, starring Frankie Howerd, Dora Bryan, Reg Varney and George Cole

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: After enjoying a trip to the theatre with Captain Hastings, the detective is disturbed by the sound of gunshots in an apartment at Whitehaven Mansions, and soon finds himself investigating a murder. Drama, starring David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson

programme.title: Princess Diana: A Life After Death
programme.desc: Diana, Princess of Wales' death set the British nation thinking about the monarchy, and a strange atmosphere existed throughout the nation in the unstable summer of 1997. Prime minister Tony Blair had just won an election with a landslide majority and the tabloid press were pursuing Diana everywhere. Events were already leading to a full-scale crisis and the sudden and unexpected death of Diana changed the country

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Foundlings are people who were abandoned as babies, often in the first hours and days of their lives and have had no way to unlock the secrets of their past. In this 90-minute special presented by Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell, new DNA technology and painstaking detective work is combined to help solve the mysteries of people with unknown beginning. The team uses an extraordinary new technique to try to identify birth families and find answers for people who have spent decades striving to find out where they came from

programme.title: Sanditon
programme.desc: Andrew Davies's adaptation of Jane Austen's unfinished final novel about a spirited young woman who finds herself in a seaside resort on the cusp of dramatic change - and becomes involved in the scheming of its ambitious inhabitants. Charlotte and Sidney find themselves at loggerheads but must work together in an attempt to rescue Miss Lambe. Tom tries to save Sanditon and his marriage, while Edward and Clara enter into a scandalous pact to finally get their hands on Lady Denham's inheritance

programme.title: Vanity Fair
programme.desc: Becky masters the fine art of living well on nothing a year, proving that a respectable public appearance is all you need to get credit. Her flirtation with Lord Steyne at least provides a reliable source of income, but the Sedley family are less fortunate. With her finances in tatters, a devastated Amelia is forced to send her son to live with the Osbournes. Becky rises to the very top of high society, even securing a royal audience - but Steyne expects something in return for his patronage, and when Rawdon is accosted by bailiffs, he seizes his chance

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Ronnie and Sam are given 10 hours to find a hostage who was taken by two armed men, following a bungled robbery in which an innocent 'have-a-go-hero' was shot dead. When one of the accused is seized, the team is offered a deal - he will tell them the location of the captive in return for immunity from prosecution. Tamzin Outhwaite guest stars as uncompromising lawyer Miriam Pescatore

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks is forced to reopen a 14-year-old case when a dying crack addict confesses to the murder of teenager Amanda Bennet, a crime for which her father Simon (guest star Tim McInnerny) was originally convicted. Brooks becomes torn between the belief of his former partner Bernie Rawlins and mounting doubts over the ability of current colleague Casey, and the case unearths the possibility that more ghosts from his drinking days will come back to haunt him. Starring Bradley Walsh and Paul Nicholls

programme.title: Davina McCall: Life at the Extreme
programme.desc: Davina travels to the Azores in the mid-Atlantic to investigate how animals cope with atmospheric pressure changes underwater, particularly the ones that have to take in air before they submerge. She swims with a school of dolphins and finds out about the inbuilt response that both animals and humans have when they go underwater, and meets a world champion freediver who teaches her the techniques needed to swim alongside sperm whales, before trying to overcome her nerves and dive down to the ocean floor on a single breath

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: English country squire Marcus Waverly receives a series of anonymous letters threatening to kidnap his son Johnnie, but the case is dismissed by Scotland Yard - prompting Poirot to open his own investigation. Drama, starring David Suchet and Geoffrey Bateman

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid gets in a flap when he learns his employer is planning to visit him at home. Family comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.desc: Belle shocks the Dingles, Vinny hits a nerve and Tracy puts pressure on Nate

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liz refuses to accept Jim's offer on the house. Vera tries to find out if Terry spent time at Tricia's flat. Martin is horrified to learn Nick has been missing for four days

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Gail contacts the police about Nick being missing, and Don thinks the Platts have driven his grandson away. Vera tells Jack that they should support Tricia

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Robert is jealous that all the talk in the Sugden household is about Andy. Mandy accuses Viv of ruining her job prospects. Rachel bans Chris from seeing Joseph

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack is worried about money. Vic is pleased to get a postcard from Kelly telling him that she is okay. Zoe decides to hand over responsibility for Home Farm business to Steve

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The Aidensfield police are puzzled when it seems a face from the past has become the unlikely victim of vandalism, and suspicions are soon aroused over the reasons for his sudden return to the village. Meanwhile, Vernon encounters dangerous wildlife as he stashes his capital in Ashfordly woods, but decides to turn the incident into another money-making scheme. Geoffrey Hughes stars

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid takes a little exercise and Trevor decides to join him. Seventies sitcom, starring Sid James and Anthony Jackson

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Mike takes up photography, and persuades Sid to let him use the old garage for his new hobby. Family comedy, starring Sid James, Diana Coupland and Robin Stewart

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: A faulty TV aerial prompts George to borrow his neighbour's ladder - but relations become strained when the henpecked husband falls off the roof and starts pointing the finger of blame. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Millionaire Gordon Cloade's wife is widowed for the second time at the tender age of 23, arousing the suspicions of his impoverished relatives. Fanning the flames of envy and hatred, psychic aunt Kathy Cloade reveals the grieving widow's first husband is in fact still alive, and a second death prompts Poirot to delve deeper into the family's dark secrets. David Suchet stars, with Jenny Agutter, Tim Piggot-Smith and Celia Imrie

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Merton faces a race against time to stop a saboteur when Aidensfield hosts a prestigious car hill-climb. Meanwhile, Bernie finds an old car in a cowshed and decides to renovate it, giving Vernon his chance to scoop the winnings in the event. Rural police drama, starring Duncan Bell, Peter Benson and Geoffrey Hughes
